),

    FROM loan 
    GROUP BY date_trunc('year', date)
),
combined AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(t.year, l.year) as year,
        COALESCE(t.trans_count, 0) as trans_count,
        COALESCE(l.loan_count, 0) as loan_count
    FROM trans_yearly t

)

    trans_count + loan_count as count
FROM combined
ORDER BY year

-- Create the function for custom quarter calculation
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION what_quarter_is(date_in date, start_month int)
RETURNS int AS $$
DECLARE
    shifted_month int;
BEGIN
    -- Shift month so that start_month becomes 1
    shifted_month := (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM date_in) - start_month + 1);
    
    -- Handle wrap-around for negative or zero values
    shifted_month := ((shifted_month - 1) % 12) + 1;
    
    -- Return quarter (1-4)
    RETURN CEIL(shifted_month / 3.0)::int;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE;

-- Example usage with the trans table
SELECT 
    trans_id,
    date,
    what_quarter_is(date, 4) as fiscal_quarter
FROM trans
LIMIT 10;

-- Alternative: inline calculation without function (for portability)
SELECT 
    trans_id,
    date,
    CEIL(((EXTRACT(MONTH FROM date) - 4 + 1 - 1) % 12 + 1) / 3.0)::int as fiscal_quarter
FROM trans
LIMIT 10

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- First, find all cards that have no previous card (starting points)
starting_cards AS (
    SELECT c.id, c.nextCardId
    FROM collection c
    LEFT JOIN collection prev ON c.id = prev.nextCardId
    WHERE prev.id IS NULL
),
-- Build paths forward from starting points
path_cte AS (
    SELECT id, nextCardId, id::TEXT AS Path
    FROM starting_cards
    UNION ALL
    SELECT c.id, c.nextCardId, p.Path || ' --> ' || c.id
    FROM collection c
    JOIN path_cte p ON c.id = p.nextCardId
)
-- Only return paths that end at cards with no next card
SELECT Path
FROM path_cte
WHERE nextCardId IS NULL
ORDER BY Path

WITH monthly_purchases AS (
    -- Get distinct months per user
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        user_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')) AS month_date
    FROM purchase
),
streaks AS (
    -- Calculate streak groups using the classic gaps-and-islands technique
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        month_date,
        month_date - INTERVAL '1 month' * ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY month_date) AS grp
    FROM monthly_purchases
),
streak_lengths AS (
    -- Calculate length of each streak
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        grp,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streaks
    GROUP BY user_id, grp
),
max_streaks AS (
    -- Get the longest streak for each user
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        MAX(streak_length) AS longest_streak
    FROM streak_lengths
    GROUP BY user_id
)
-- Count how many users have each longest streak length
SELECT 
    longest_streak AS streak_length,
    COUNT(*) AS num_users
FROM max_streaks
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY longest_streak

SELECT
    (SELECT r.relname FROM pg_class r WHERE r.oid = c.confrelid) AS base_table,
    a_base.attname AS base_col,
    (SELECT r.relname FROM pg_class r WHERE r.oid = c.conrelid) AS referencing_table,
    a_ref.attname AS referencing_col,
    pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) AS constraint_sql
FROM pg_constraint c
JOIN LATERAL UNNEST(c.confkey) WITH ORDINALITY AS base_keys(attnum, ord) ON TRUE
JOIN LATERAL UNNEST(c.conkey) WITH ORDINALITY AS ref_keys(attnum, ord) 
    ON base_keys.ord = ref_keys.ord
JOIN pg_attribute a_base 
    ON a_base.attrelid = c.confrelid AND a_base.attnum = base_keys.attnum
JOIN pg_attribute a_ref 
    ON a_ref.attrelid = c.conrelid AND a_ref.attnum = ref_keys.attnum
WHERE c.confrelid = (SELECT oid FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'users')
  AND c.confrelid != c.conrelid
  AND c.contype = 'f'

WITH distinct_dates AS (
    -- First, get distinct release dates
    SELECT DISTINCT releasedate
    FROM sets_releaseinfo
),
numbered AS (
    -- Assign row numbers and calculate a "group identifier"
    SELECT 
        releasedate,
        releasedate - (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY releasedate) * INTERVAL '1 day') AS grp
    FROM distinct_dates
),
groups AS (
    -- Group by the calculated group identifier to find consecutive sequences
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS count,
        MIN(releasedate) AS start_date,
        MAX(releasedate) AS end_date
    FROM numbered
    GROUP BY grp
)
-- Filter for periods with more than 1 day and order by count descending
SELECT 
    count,
    start_date,
    end_date
FROM groups
WHERE count > 1
ORDER BY count DESC, start_date

WITH RankedTransactions AS (
  -- Rank transactions per customer per gas station by date/time (latest first)
  SELECT
    *,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC) AS rn_per_station,
    -- Check if this transaction has amount < 10
    CASE WHEN amount < 10 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_issue
  FROM transactions_1k
),
LastPerStation AS (
  -- Keep only the last transaction per gas station
  SELECT *
  FROM RankedTransactions
  WHERE rn_per_station = 1
),
CustomerIssues AS (
  -- Find the first gas station (chronologically) where amount < 10 occurred for each customer
  SELECT
    customerid,
    gasstationid AS first_issue_gasstation
  FROM (
    SELECT
      customerid,
      gasstationid,
      ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid ORDER BY date, time) AS rn_issue
    FROM LastPerStation
    WHERE amount < 10
  ) sub
  WHERE rn_issue = 1
),
CustomerLastValid AS (
  -- Find the last gas station where amount >= 10 for customers with no issues
  SELECT
    customerid,
    gasstationid AS last_valid_gasstation
  FROM (
    SELECT
      customerid,
      gasstationid,
      ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC) AS rn_last
    FROM LastPerStation
    WHERE amount >= 10
  ) sub
  WHERE rn_last = 1
)
SELECT
  lps.customerid,
  lps.transactionid,
  lps.gasstationid,
  lps.amount
FROM LastPerStation lps
LEFT JOIN CustomerIssues ci ON lps.customerid = ci.customerid
LEFT JOIN CustomerLastValid clv ON lps.customerid = clv.customerid
WHERE
  -- Case 1: Customer has an issue, show that first issue gas station
  (ci.first_issue_gasstation IS NOT NULL AND lps.gasstationid = ci.first_issue_gasstation)
  -- Case 2: No issue, show last valid gas station
  OR (ci.first_issue_gasstation IS NULL AND lps.gasstationid = clv.last_valid_gasstation)

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Get all subgroup memberships (treating both subgroup columns similarly)
subgroup_members AS (
    -- Items from subgroup1
    SELECT id AS item, subgroup1 AS subgroup 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
    UNION ALL
    -- Items from subgroup2  
    SELECT id AS item, subgroup2 AS subgroup 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
),
-- Find all pairs of items that share a subgroup (edges in the graph)
shared_subgroups AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT a.item AS item1, b.item AS item2
    FROM subgroup_members a
    JOIN subgroup_members b ON a.subgroup = b.subgroup AND a.item < b.item
),
-- Build adjacency list (all connected items for each item)
connections AS (
    SELECT item1 AS item, item2 AS connected_item FROM shared_subgroups
    UNION ALL
    SELECT item2 AS item, item1 AS connected_item FROM shared_subgroups
),
-- Get all unique items
all_items AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT item FROM subgroup_members
),
-- Recursive CTE to find connected components
recursive_groups AS (
    -- Base case: each item starts as its own group (represented by minimum item in component)
    SELECT item, item AS root, 0 AS depth
    FROM all_items
    
    UNION
    
    -- Recursive case: follow connections to find all reachable items
    SELECT c.connected_item, 
           LEAST(r.root, c.connected_item) AS root,
           r.depth + 1
    FROM recursive_groups r
    JOIN connections c ON r.item = c.item
    WHERE r.depth < 100  -- Prevent infinite loops
),
-- Get the minimum root for each item (final group representative)
final_roots AS (
    SELECT item, MIN(root) AS group_root
    FROM recursive_groups
    GROUP BY item
),
-- Original queries for reference
qr1 AS (
    SELECT id AS item, subgroup1 AS subgroup 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
),
qr2 AS (
    SELECT id AS item, subgroup2 AS subgroup 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
)
-- Final result with group assignment
SELECT 
    COALESCE(qr1.item, qr2.item) AS item,
    qr1.subgroup AS subgroup1,
    qr2.subgroup AS subgroup2,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY fr.group_root) AS group_id
FROM qr1
FULL OUTER JOIN qr2 ON qr1.item = qr2.item
LEFT JOIN final_roots fr ON fr.item = COALESCE(qr1.item, qr2.item)
ORDER BY group_id, item

months AS (

WITH ordered_activities AS (
    SELECT 
        ts,
        superhero_id,
        activity_code,
        LAG(ts) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) as prev_ts
    FROM superhero_activities
),
group_markers AS (
    SELECT 
        ts,
        superhero_id,
        activity_code,
        CASE 
            WHEN prev_ts IS NULL OR ts - prev_ts > INTERVAL '5 minutes' 
            THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END as is_new_group
    FROM ordered_activities
),
grouped AS (
    SELECT 
        ts,
        superhero_id,
        activity_code,
        SUM(is_new_group) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) as group_id
    FROM group_markers
)
SELECT 
    superhero_id,
    activity_code,
    MIN(ts) as interval_start,
    MAX(ts) as interval_end,
    (MAX(ts) - MIN(ts)) + INTERVAL '5 minutes' as total_duration
FROM grouped
GROUP BY superhero_id, activity_code, group_id
ORDER BY superhero_id, activity_code, interval_start

WITH RECURSIVE league_hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: Start with the specific league
    SELECT 
        id as league_id,
        name,
        country_id
    FROM league
    WHERE id = 1  -- Replace with your specific league_id
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: This would need a parent_id column in league table
    -- Since no such column exists in the schema, this demonstrates proper syntax
    SELECT 
        l.id,
        l.name,
        l.country_id
    FROM league l
    JOIN league_hierarchy lh ON l.country_id = lh.country_id  -- Example join condition
    WHERE l.id != lh.league_id
)
SELECT DISTINCT
    lh.league_id,
    lh.name as league_name,
    t.team_api_id,
    t.team_long_name as team_name
FROM league_hierarchy lh
JOIN match m ON m.league_id = lh.league_id
JOIN team t ON t.team_api_id = m.home_team_api_id OR t.team_api_id = m.away_team_api_id

),
filtered_artists AS (

    FROM artist_set_counts
    WHERE total_cards > 1
),
ranked_sets AS (
    SELECT 
        artist,
        setcode,
        cards_in_set,
        total_cards,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY artist ORDER BY cards_in_set DESC) AS set_rank
    FROM filtered_artists
)

    cards_in_set AS counter,
    CASE WHEN set_rank = 1 THEN 'Top Set' ELSE NULL END AS is_top_set
FROM ranked_sets
ORDER BY total_cards DESC, artist, cards_in_set DESC

WITH RECURSIVE ancestor_chain AS (
    -- Base case: start with the given atom
    SELECT atom_id, parent_id, enabled, 0 as depth
    FROM connection 
    WHERE atom_id = 4
    
    UNION ALL 
    
    -- Recursive case: find parents of current atoms
    SELECT c.atom_id, c.parent_id, c.enabled, ac.depth + 1
    FROM connection c 
    INNER JOIN ancestor_chain ac ON c.atom_id = ac.parent_id
    WHERE ac.parent_id IS NOT NULL
),
-- Check if any ancestor (depth > 0) has enabled = false or NULL
disabled_check AS (
    SELECT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM ancestor_chain WHERE depth > 0 AND (enabled = false OR enabled IS NULL)
    ) as has_disabled_ancestor
)
-- Return ancestors only if no disabled ancestors found
SELECT ac.atom_id, ac.parent_id, ac.enabled
FROM ancestor_chain ac, disabled_check dc
WHERE ac.depth > 0 AND dc.has_disabled_ancestor = false

WITH max_date_values AS (
    SELECT id, MAX(date) AS max_date
    FROM laboratory
    GROUP BY id
),
max_got_gpt AS (
    SELECT a.id, a.date AS max_date, a.got AS max_got, a.gpt AS max_gpt
    FROM laboratory a
    INNER JOIN max_date_values b ON a.id = b.id AND a.date = b.max_date
),
matching_min_dates AS (
    SELECT 
        a.id,
        MIN(a.date) AS min_date,
        b.max_got,
        b.max_gpt
    FROM laboratory a
    INNER JOIN max_got_gpt b ON a.id = b.id

)

FROM laboratory a
INNER JOIN matching_min_dates b ON a.id = b.id AND a.date = b.min_date
ORDER BY a.id, a.date

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() 
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$ 
BEGIN 
    -- Update the previous record's eff_to to be one day before the new record's date
    UPDATE team_attributes 
    SET eff_to = TO_DATE(NEW.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') - 1
    WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
      AND eff_to IS NULL
      AND id != NEW.id;
    
    -- Set the new record's eff_to to the default far future date
    NEW.eff_to := TO_DATE('5999-12-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD');
    
    RETURN NEW;
END; 
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER before_insert_team_attributes 
BEFORE INSERT ON team_attributes 
FOR EACH ROW 
EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to()

WITH distinct_drivers_per_position AS (
    -- Get distinct driver-position combinations (a driver finishing same position in multiple races counts once)
    SELECT DISTINCT position, driverid 
    FROM results 
    WHERE position IS NOT NULL
),
ranked_drivers AS (
    -- Rank drivers within each position group by driverid descending to identify highest
    SELECT 
        position, 
        driverid,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY position ORDER BY driverid DESC) as rn_desc,
        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY position) as driver_count
    FROM distinct_drivers_per_position
)
-- Select all drivers except the highest driverid in each position group
-- Only include positions where more than one distinct driver had that position
SELECT driverid 
FROM ranked_drivers 
WHERE rn_desc > 1  -- Exclude the highest driverid (rn_desc = 1)
AND driver_count > 1  -- Only positions with multiple distinct drivers
ORDER BY position, driverid

WITH RECURSIVE ManagerChain AS (
    -- Base case: start with each employee as their own starting point
    SELECT 
        id as employee_id,
        salary as employee_salary,
        bossid,
        salary as boss_salary,
        0 as hierarchical_level
    FROM staff
    WHERE bossid IS NOT NULL  -- Only employees who have a boss
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: climb up the hierarchy
    SELECT 
        mc.employee_id,
        mc.employee_salary,
        s.bossid,
        s.salary as boss_salary,
        mc.hierarchical_level + 1
    FROM ManagerChain mc
    INNER JOIN staff s ON mc.bossid = s.id
    WHERE mc.bossid IS NOT NULL  -- Continue until we reach the top
),
QualifiedManagers AS (
    -- Find all managers who earn at least twice as much as the employee
    SELECT 
        employee_id,
        employee_salary,
        hierarchical_level,
        bossid as boss_id,
        boss_salary,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY employee_id ORDER BY hierarchical_level) as rn
    FROM ManagerChain
    WHERE boss_salary >= 2 * employee_salary
      AND hierarchical_level > 0  -- Must be an indirect manager (not themselves)
)
SELECT 
    employee_id as employeeId,
    employee_salary as employeeSalary,
    hierarchical_level as hierarchical_level,
    boss_id as bossId,
    boss_salary as bossSalary
FROM QualifiedManagers
WHERE rn = 1  -- Get the lowest-ranked (closest) manager meeting the criteria
ORDER BY employee_id

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
    -- Base case: start with the first row
    SELECT 
        id, 
        amount, 
        amount AS cum_sum, 
        1 AS bucket
    FROM transfer
    WHERE id = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: join with next row
    SELECT 
        t.id,
        t.amount,
        CASE 
            WHEN c.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN t.amount  -- reset to current amount
            ELSE c.cum_sum + t.amount                       -- continue accumulating
        END AS cum_sum,
        CASE 
            WHEN c.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN c.bucket + 1  -- new bucket
            ELSE c.bucket                                      -- same bucket
        END AS bucket
    FROM cte c
    JOIN transfer t ON t.id = c.id + 1
)
SELECT id, amount, cum_sum, bucket
FROM cte
ORDER BY id

SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS yesterday_revenue,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) - 
         SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 8 THEN amount ELSE 0 END))::numeric / 
        NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 8 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0), 
        2
    ) AS pct_change_week_ago,
    SUM(CASE WHEN date_trunc('month', timestamp) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) 
              AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS mtd,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN date_trunc('month', timestamp) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) 
                  AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) - 
         SUM(CASE WHEN date_trunc('month', timestamp) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) - interval '1 month' THEN amount ELSE 0 END))::numeric / 
        NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN date_trunc('month', timestamp) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) - interval '1 month' THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0), 
        2
    ) AS pct_change_month_prior 
FROM transactions

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION calculate_loan_status()
    RETURNS SETOF loan_last
    AS $$
    BEGIN
        -- Direct insert from loan_first to loan_last
        INSERT INTO loan_last 
        SELECT loan_id, status 
        FROM loan_first 
        WHERE status = 'A';
        
        -- Return all records from loan_last
        RETURN QUERY SELECT * FROM loan_last;
    END;
    $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql

WITH cte_funding_count AS (
    SELECT fundingtype, COUNT(*) AS count 
    FROM schools 
    GROUP BY fundingtype
),
total AS (
    SELECT SUM(count) AS total_count 
    FROM cte_funding_count
),
cte_with_percent AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype, 
        count, 
        ROUND(count * 100.0 / total.total_count, 0) AS percent
    FROM cte_funding_count
    CROSS JOIN total
),
adjustment AS (
    SELECT 
        100 - SUM(percent) AS diff
    FROM cte_with_percent
)
SELECT 
    fundingtype, 
    count,

WITH hero_attribute(hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_value) AS (
    VALUES (1, 1, 80), (2, 1, 75), (3, 1, 95), (4, 1, 85), (5, 1, 90), (6, 1, 70)
),
-- Identify center groups (n=2 rows each)
center_groups AS (
    SELECT hero_id, attribute_value, (hero_id - 1) / 2 AS center_group
    FROM hero_attribute
),
-- Get the center group boundaries
center_ranges AS (
    SELECT center_group, MIN(hero_id) AS center_start, MAX(hero_id) AS center_end
    FROM center_groups
    GROUP BY center_group
),
-- Expand each center with k=2 previous and k'=2 next
expanded_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        cr.center_group,
        ha.hero_id,
        ha.attribute_value
    FROM center_ranges cr
    JOIN hero_attribute ha 
        ON ha.hero_id >= cr.center_start - 2  -- k=2 previous
        AND ha.hero_id <= cr.center_end + 2   -- k'=2 next
    WHERE ha.hero_id >= 1  -- ensure we don't go below 1
)
SELECT SUM(attribute_value) AS sum
FROM expanded_groups
GROUP BY center_group
ORDER BY center_group

    CASE WHEN c.uid = s.uid THEN 1 ELSE 0 END, 
    c.msg 
FROM words_chat c 
JOIN words_games g USING (gid) 
JOIN words_social s ON s.uid IN (g.player1, g.player2) 
JOIN words_users u ON u.uid = c.uid
WHERE c.gid = in_gid 
    AND s.social = in_social

ORDER BY c.created ASC;

WITH date_series AS (
    SELECT generate_series('2022-01-01'::timestamp, '2022-01-31'::timestamp, '1 day'::interval)::date AS day_start
),
incidents_converted AS (
    SELECT 
        to_timestamp(incident_start / 1000.0) AS incident_start_ts,
        to_timestamp(incident_end / 1000.0) AS incident_end_ts
    FROM race_incidents
    WHERE incident_start IS NOT NULL AND incident_end IS NOT NULL
),
daily_minutes AS (
    SELECT 
        ds.day_start,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (
            LEAST(ic.incident_end_ts, ds.day_start::timestamp + interval '1 day')
            - GREATEST(ic.incident_start_ts, ds.day_start::timestamp)
        )) / 60.0 AS minutes
    FROM date_series ds
    JOIN incidents_converted ic 
        ON ic.incident_start_ts < ds.day_start::timestamp + interval '1 day'
        AND ic.incident_end_ts > ds.day_start::timestamp
)
SELECT 
    day_start,
    COALESCE(SUM(GREATEST(minutes, 0))::integer, 0) AS incident_minutes
FROM daily_minutes
GROUP BY day_start
ORDER BY day_start

WITH filtered_budget AS (
    -- Filter for only Food and Advertisement categories
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        -- Assign priority: Food = 1, Advertisement = 2
        CASE 
            WHEN category = 'Food' THEN 1
            WHEN category = 'Advertisement' THEN 2
            ELSE 3
        END AS priority
    FROM budget
    WHERE category IN ('Food', 'Advertisement')
      AND amount IS NOT NULL
),
ordered_budget AS (
    -- Order by priority first, then by amount (to maximize number of items, use smaller amounts first)
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        priority,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY priority, amount) AS item_order
    FROM filtered_budget
),
cumulative_calc AS (
    -- Calculate cumulative sum across all items (not partitioned by category)
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        priority,
        item_order,
        SUM(amount) OVER (ORDER BY priority, amount ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS cum_amount
    FROM ordered_budget
)
SELECT 
    budget_id,
    category,
    amount,
    cum_amount,
    -- Show which items can be purchased within 500 budget
    CASE 
        WHEN cum_amount <= 500 THEN 'Purchased'
        ELSE 'Not Purchased'
    END AS purchase_status,
    -- Show remaining budget after this item
    500 - cum_amount AS remaining_after
FROM cumulative_calc
ORDER BY priority, amount

WITH RECURSIVE ordered_rows AS (
    SELECT id, trx, event, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) as rn
    FROM sales_transactions
),
calc AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id,
        trx,
        event,
        ROUND((trx * event)::numeric, 6) as result,
        trx as cum_trx,
        ROUND((trx * event)::numeric, 6) as cum_result,
        rn
    FROM ordered_rows
    WHERE rn = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case
    SELECT 
        o.id,
        o.trx,
        o.event,
        ROUND(((o.trx + c.cum_trx + c.cum_result) * o.event)::numeric, 6) as result,
        c.cum_trx + o.trx as cum_trx,
        c.cum_result + ROUND(((o.trx + c.cum_trx + c.cum_result) * o.event)::numeric, 6) as cum_result,
        o.rn
    FROM calc c
    JOIN ordered_rows o ON o.rn = c.rn + 1
)
SELECT id, result FROM calc ORDER BY id

WITH ranked_prices AS (
    -- Rank prices within each day for each card
    SELECT 
        card_id,
        DATE(date_value) AS trade_date,
        date_value,
        price,
        date_value - MIN(date_value) OVER (PARTITION BY card_id, DATE(date_value)) AS time_from_start,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY card_id, DATE(date_value) ORDER BY date_value) AS rn_asc,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY card_id, DATE(date_value) ORDER BY date_value DESC) AS rn_desc
    FROM card_prices
),
first_prices AS (
    -- Get first price of each day
    SELECT card_id, trade_date, price AS first_price
    FROM ranked_prices
    WHERE rn_asc = 1
),
last_in_15min AS (
    -- Get last price within first 15 minutes (or closest to it)
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (card_id, trade_date)
        card_id,
        trade_date,
        price AS last_price_in_window
    FROM ranked_prices
    WHERE time_from_start <= INTERVAL '15 minutes'
    ORDER BY card_id, trade_date, date_value DESC
),
daily_differences AS (
    -- Join first and last prices, calculate difference
    SELECT 
        f.card_id,
        f.trade_date,
        l.last_price_in_window - f.first_price AS price_diff
    FROM first_prices f
    INNER JOIN last_in_15min l ON f.card_id = l.card_id AND f.trade_date = l.trade_date
)
SELECT 
    card_id,
    AVG(price_diff) AS avg_difference
FROM daily_differences
GROUP BY card_id
ORDER BY card_id

WITH sales_with_attributes AS (
    SELECT 
        dp.id AS datapoint_id,
        dp.value,
        dp.time_dimension_id,
        dp.spatial_dimension_id,
        MAX(CASE WHEN v.name = 'ProductType' THEN vv.name END) AS product_type,
        MAX(CASE WHEN v.name = 'publishing_language' THEN vv.name END) AS language,
        MAX(CASE WHEN v.name = 'genre' THEN vv.name END) AS genre
    FROM datapoints dp
    JOIN datapoint_variablevalue dpvv ON dp.id = dpvv.datapoint_id
    JOIN variablevalue vv ON dpvv.variablevalue_id = vv.id
    JOIN variable v ON vv.variable_id = v.id
    GROUP BY dp.id, dp.value, dp.time_dimension_id, dp.spatial_dimension_id
),
all_combinations AS (
    SELECT 
        td.years,
        sd.name AS country,
        pt.name AS product_type,
        pl.name AS language,
        g.name AS genre
    FROM time_dimension td
    CROSS JOIN spatial_dimension sd
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT name FROM variablevalue WHERE variable_id = (SELECT id FROM variable WHERE name = 'ProductType')) pt
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT name FROM variablevalue WHERE variable_id = (SELECT id FROM variable WHERE name = 'publishing_language')) pl
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT name FROM variablevalue WHERE variable_id = (SELECT id FROM variable WHERE name = 'genre')) g
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(SUM(swa.value), 0) AS total_sales,
    ac.years,
    ac.country,
    ac.product_type,
    ac.language,
    ac.genre
FROM all_combinations ac
LEFT JOIN sales_with_attributes swa ON 
    ac.years = swa.years AND
    ac.country = swa.country AND
    ac.product_type = swa.product_type AND
    ac.language = swa.language AND
    ac.genre = swa.genre
GROUP BY ac.years, ac.country, ac.product_type, ac.language, ac.genre
ORDER BY ac.years DESC, ac.country, ac.product_type, ac.language, ac.genre

WITH params AS (
    SELECT 
        TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36' AS start_ts,
        TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24' AS end_ts
),
day_intervals AS (
    SELECT 
        d::date AS day_date,
        EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM d) AS dow,
        CASE 
            WHEN d::date = DATE(p.start_ts) THEN p.start_ts
            ELSE d::date
        END AS interval_start,
        CASE 
            WHEN d::date = DATE(p.end_ts) THEN p.end_ts
            ELSE d::date + INTERVAL '1 day'
        END AS interval_end
    FROM params p,
         generate_series(
             DATE(p.start_ts),
             DATE(p.end_ts),
             INTERVAL '1 day'
         ) AS d
    WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM d) < 6
)
SELECT 
    (FLOOR(total_seconds / 86400))::int || ' days ' ||
    (FLOOR((total_seconds % 86400) / 3600))::int || ' hours ' ||
    (FLOOR(((total_seconds % 86400) % 3600) / 60))::int || ' minutes ' ||
    (FLOOR(((total_seconds % 86400) % 3600) % 60))::int || ' seconds' AS time_diff
FROM (
    SELECT SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (interval_end - interval_start))) AS total_seconds 
    FROM day_intervals
) t

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- First, enumerate all buy and sell transactions with running totals
buy_transactions AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_id,
        molecule_id,
        date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) AS cumulative_molecules,
        molecules AS remaining_molecules
    FROM transaction
    WHERE sell = 0
),
sell_transactions AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_id,
        molecule_id,
        date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) AS cumulative_molecules
    FROM transaction
    WHERE sell = 1
),
-- Create ranges for matching: each buy covers (prev_cumulative+1) to (cumulative)
buy_ranges AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_id,
        molecule_id,
        date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        cumulative_molecules - molecules + 1 AS range_start,
        cumulative_molecules AS range_end,
        usd_total * 1.0 / molecules AS unit_price
    FROM buy_transactions
),
sell_ranges AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_id,
        molecule_id,
        date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        cumulative_molecules - molecules + 1 AS range_start,
        cumulative_molecules AS range_end,
        usd_total * 1.0 / molecules AS unit_price
    FROM sell_transactions
),
-- Find all overlapping buy-sell pairs
overlaps AS (
    SELECT 
        b.molecule_id,
        b.date AS buy_date,
        s.date AS sell_date,
        b.unit_price AS buy_unit_price,
        s.unit_price AS sell_unit_price,
        GREATEST(b.range_start, s.range_start) AS overlap_start,
        LEAST(b.range_end, s.range_end) AS overlap_end
    FROM buy_ranges b
    JOIN sell_ranges s ON b.molecule_id = s.molecule_id 
        AND b.range_start <= s.range_end 
        AND b.range_end >= s.range_start
)
SELECT 
    molecule_id,
    buy_date,
    sell_date,
    overlap_end - overlap_start + 1 AS molecules,
    ROUND((overlap_end - overlap_start + 1) * buy_unit_price, 2) AS buy_usd_total,
    ROUND((overlap_end - overlap_start + 1) * sell_unit_price, 2) AS sell_usd_total
FROM overlaps
ORDER BY molecule_id, buy_date, sell_date

SELECT
  begin_date,
  end_date,
  CASE
    WHEN start_month > end_month THEN 0
    ELSE (
      (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM end_month)::int * 12 + EXTRACT(MONTH FROM end_month)::int)
      - (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM start_month)::int * 12 + EXTRACT(MONTH FROM start_month)::int)
      + 1
    )
  END AS full_calendar_months
FROM (
  SELECT
    begin_date,
    end_date,
    -- first full month to count (include start month only if it starts on the 1st)
    (date_trunc('month', begin_date)::date
       + CASE WHEN begin_date = date_trunc('month', begin_date)::date
              THEN interval '0 month'
              ELSE interval '1 month'
         END
    )::date AS start_month,
    -- last full month to count (exclude the month containing end_date)
    (date_trunc('month', end_date)::date - interval '1 month')::date AS end_month
  FROM race_dates
) m;

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- First, get all unique ref values and map them to user ids
user_refs AS (
    SELECT id, username, ref1 AS ref FROM users WHERE ref1 IS NOT NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT id, username, ref2 AS ref FROM users WHERE ref2 IS NOT NULL
),
-- Find connected components using recursive traversal
connected AS (
    -- Start with each user as their own component (using min id as component id)
    SELECT 
        id AS user_id,
        id AS component_root,
        ref1,
        ref2,
        username
    FROM users
    UNION ALL
    -- Recursively find users connected through shared refs
    SELECT 
        u.id AS user_id,
        LEAST(c.component_root, u.id) AS component_root,
        u.ref1,
        u.ref2,
        u.username
    FROM users u
    JOIN connected c ON (
        (u.ref1 IS NOT NULL AND (u.ref1 = c.ref1 OR u.ref1 = c.ref2))
        OR (u.ref2 IS NOT NULL AND (u.ref2 = c.ref1 OR u.ref2 = c.ref2))
    )
    WHERE u.id <> c.user_id
),
-- Get final component assignment (minimum root for each user)
final_components AS (
    SELECT user_id, MIN(component_root) AS component_root
    FROM connected
    GROUP BY user_id
),
-- Aggregate users by component
merged_users AS (
    SELECT 
        fc.component_root,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.id) AS ids,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.username) AS usernames,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref1) FILTER (WHERE u.ref1 IS NOT NULL) AS refs1,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref2) FILTER (WHERE u.ref2 IS NOT NULL) AS refs2
    FROM final_components fc
    JOIN users u ON fc.user_id = u.id
    GROUP BY fc.component_root
),
-- Get all refs for each component to match with assets
component_refs AS (
    SELECT 
        component_root,
        UNNEST(refs1 || refs2) AS ref
    FROM merged_users
),
-- Count assets for each component
asset_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        cr.component_root,
        COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_count,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_ids
    FROM component_refs cr
    LEFT JOIN assets a ON a.owner = cr.ref
    GROUP BY cr.component_root
)
-- Final result: merged users with their asset counts
SELECT 
    mu.ids,
    mu.usernames,
    mu.refs1,
    mu.refs2,
    COALESCE(ac.asset_count, 0) AS asset_count,
    COALESCE(ac.asset_ids, ARRAY[]::integer[]) AS asset_ids
FROM merged_users mu
LEFT JOIN asset_counts ac ON mu.component_root = ac.component_root

UNION ALL

-- Handle assets with owners not in users table
SELECT 
    ARRAY[]::integer[] AS ids,
    ARRAY[]::text[] AS usernames,
    ARRAY[]::text[] AS refs1,
    ARRAY[a.owner] AS refs2,
    COUNT(*)::integer AS asset_count,
    ARRAY_AGG(a.id) AS asset_ids
FROM assets a
WHERE a.owner NOT IN (SELECT ref1 FROM users WHERE ref1 IS NOT NULL)
  AND a.owner NOT IN (SELECT ref2 FROM users WHERE ref2 IS NOT NULL)
GROUP BY a.owner

ORDER BY asset_count DESC, ids

SELECT 
    CD.customer_id,
    COALESCE(S.total_sales, 0) AS total_sales,
    COALESCE(S.total_amount, 0) AS total_amount,
    COALESCE(C.total_communications, 0) AS total_communications,
    COALESCE(F.total_feedback, 0) AS total_feedback,
    COALESCE(F.total_positive_feedback, 0) AS total_positive_feedback,
    COALESCE(F.total_negative_feedback, 0) AS total_negative_feedback
FROM customer_details CD
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS total_sales, SUM(amount) AS total_amount
    FROM sales
    GROUP BY customer_id
) S ON CD.customer_id = S.customer_id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS total_communications
    FROM communications
    GROUP BY customer_id
) C ON CD.customer_id = C.customer_id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        customer_id, 
        COUNT(*) AS total_feedback,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE feedback_type = 'positive') AS total_positive_feedback,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE feedback_type = 'negative') AS total_negative_feedback
    FROM feedback
    GROUP BY customer_id
) F ON CD.customer_id = F.customer_id

DO $$
DECLARE
    rec RECORD;
    sql_stmt TEXT;
BEGIN
    FOR rec IN 
        SELECT 
            quote_ident(c.table_schema) || '.' || quote_ident(c.table_name) AS full_table_name,
            quote_ident(c.column_name) AS old_col_name,
            quote_ident(lower(c.column_name)) AS new_col_name
        FROM information_schema.columns c
        WHERE c.table_schema NOT IN ('information_schema', 'pg_catalog')
          AND c.column_name <> lower(c.column_name)
        ORDER BY c.table_schema, c.table_name, c.column_name
    LOOP
        sql_stmt := 'ALTER TABLE ' || rec.full_table_name || 
                    ' RENAME COLUMN ' || rec.old_col_name || 
                    ' TO ' || rec.new_col_name || ';';
        
        RAISE NOTICE 'Executing: %', sql_stmt;
        EXECUTE sql_stmt;
    END LOOP;
END $$

WITH RECURSIVE numbered AS (
    SELECT 
        prediction_code,
        Performance_Prediction,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY prediction_code ASC) AS rn
    FROM performance_predictions
),
A(GroupNum, RollingSum, rn, prediction_code, Performance_Prediction) AS (
    SELECT 
        1, 
        Performance_Prediction, 
        rn,
        prediction_code,
        Performance_Prediction
    FROM numbered
    WHERE rn = 1
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        CASE
            WHEN A.RollingSum + n.Performance_Prediction > 200
            THEN A.GroupNum + 1
            ELSE A.GroupNum
        END,
        CASE
            WHEN A.RollingSum + n.Performance_Prediction > 200
            THEN n.Performance_Prediction
            ELSE A.RollingSum + n.Performance_Prediction
        END,
        n.rn,
        n.prediction_code,
        n.Performance_Prediction
    FROM A
    JOIN numbered AS n ON A.rn + 1 = n.rn
)
SELECT
    GroupNum,
    MAX(RollingSum) AS cumulative_sum
FROM A
GROUP BY GroupNum
ORDER BY GroupNum

SELECT

FROM transactions_1k t1
JOIN transactions_1k t2 
    ON t1.date = t2.date 
    AND t1.gasstationid = t2.gasstationid
    AND t1.customerid < t2.customerid
GROUP BY 
    LEAST(t1.customerid, t2.customerid),
    GREATEST(t1.customerid, t2.customerid)
ORDER BY shared_days DESC

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION reindex_superhero_ids(IN p_publisher_id BIGINT) 
RETURNS VOID 
LANGUAGE plpgsql 
AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Create temporary sequence
    CREATE TEMPORARY SEQUENCE IF NOT EXISTS id_seq_temp MINVALUE 1 START WITH 1 INCREMENT BY 1;
    
    -- Reset the sequence to start from 1
    ALTER SEQUENCE id_seq_temp RESTART WITH 1;
    
    -- Update hair_colour_ids to be contiguous, ordered by current hair_colour_id then id for determinism
    UPDATE superhero 
    SET hair_colour_id = nextval('id_seq_temp') 
    WHERE publisher_id = p_publisher_id
    ORDER BY hair_colour_id, id;
    
    -- Clean up the temporary sequence
    DROP SEQUENCE IF EXISTS id_seq_temp;
END;
$$

SELECT 
    e.event_id,
    e.event_name,
    COALESCE(expense_summary.total_expense, 0) AS total_expense,
    COALESCE(budget_summary.total_budget, 0) AS total_budget
FROM event e
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT b.link_to_event, SUM(e.cost) AS total_expense
    FROM expense e
    JOIN budget b ON e.link_to_budget = b.budget_id
    GROUP BY b.link_to_event
) expense_summary ON e.event_id = expense_summary.link_to_event
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT link_to_event, SUM(amount) AS total_budget
    FROM budget
    GROUP BY link_to_event
) budget_summary ON e.event_id = budget_summary.link_to_event
WHERE expense_summary.link_to_event IS NOT NULL OR budget_summary.link_to_event IS NOT NULL

SELECT 
    e.event_id,
    e.event_name,
    -- Highest attendee member ID overall
    (SELECT MAX(link_to_member) FROM attendance WHERE link_to_event = 'rec2N69DMcrqN9PJC') AS highest_attendee,
    
    -- Highest attendee with expenses and their average expense cost
    (SELECT MAX(a.link_to_member) 
     FROM attendance a 
     WHERE a.link_to_event = 'rec2N69DMcrqN9PJC' 
     AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM expense ex WHERE ex.link_to_member = a.link_to_member)
    ) AS highest_attendee_with_expenses,
    
    (SELECT AVG(ex.cost) 
     FROM expense ex 
     WHERE ex.link_to_member = (
         SELECT MAX(a.link_to_member) 
         FROM attendance a 
         WHERE a.link_to_event = 'rec2N69DMcrqN9PJC' 
         AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM expense ex2 WHERE ex2.link_to_member = a.link_to_member)
     )
    ) AS avg_expense_cost,
    
    -- Highest attendee with income and their average income amount
    (SELECT MAX(a.link_to_member) 
     FROM attendance a 
     WHERE a.link_to_event = 'rec2N69DMcrqN9PJC' 
     AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM income inc WHERE inc.link_to_member = a.link_to_member)
    ) AS highest_attendee_with_income,
    
    (SELECT AVG(inc.amount) 
     FROM income inc 
     WHERE inc.link_to_member = (
         SELECT MAX(a.link_to_member) 
         FROM attendance a 
         WHERE a.link_to_event = 'rec2N69DMcrqN9PJC' 
         AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM income inc2 WHERE inc2.link_to_member = a.link_to_member)
     )
    ) AS avg_income_amount
    
FROM event e
WHERE e.event_id = 'rec2N69DMcrqN9PJC'

WITH month_data AS (
    SELECT 
        date,
        date_trunc('month', date) as month_start,
        (date_trunc('month', date) + ((7 - EXTRACT(DOW FROM date_trunc('month', date))) % 7)::int) as first_sunday
    FROM laboratory 
    WHERE date_part('year', date) = date_part('year', CURRENT_DATE) 
      AND date_part('month', date) = date_part('month', CURRENT_DATE)
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN date <= first_sunday THEN 1
        ELSE 2 + ((date - first_sunday - 1) / 7)::int
    END as admin_week,
    COUNT(*) as record_count
FROM month_data
GROUP BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN date <= first_sunday THEN 1
        ELSE 2 + ((date - first_sunday - 1) / 7)::int
    END
ORDER BY admin_week

ALTER TABLE curricula
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS status TEXT DEFAULT 'installed';


WITH RECURSIVE curricula_to_upgrade AS (
    -- Base case: start with the target curriculum
    SELECT c.curriculum_id
    FROM curricula c
    WHERE c.curriculum_id = 1
      AND c.status = 'installed'

    UNION ALL

    -- Recursive case: find curricula that share resources with already-found curricula
    -- A curriculum depends on another if they share the same resource_key
    SELECT cr2.curriculum_id
    FROM curricula_to_upgrade ctu
    -- First, find all resources used by curricula in our set
    JOIN curriculum_resources cr1 ON cr1.curriculum_id = ctu.curriculum_id
    -- Then, find all other curricula that use those same resources
    JOIN curriculum_resources cr2 ON cr2.resource_key = cr1.resource_key
    -- Join back to curricula to ensure we only get valid curriculum_ids
    JOIN curricula c ON c.curriculum_id = cr2.curriculum_id
    WHERE c.status = 'installed'
      -- Prevent infinite loop by excluding already-found curricula
      AND cr2.curriculum_id NOT IN (SELECT curriculum_id FROM curricula_to_upgrade)
)
UPDATE curricula
SET status = 'to upgrade'
WHERE curriculum_id IN (
    SELECT curriculum_id
    FROM curricula_to_upgrade
)

WITH OrderedTransactions AS (

        transactionid,
        date,
        time,
        customerid,
        cardid,
        gasstationid,
        productid,
        amount,
        price,
        LAG(date) OVER (PARTITION BY customerid ORDER BY date, time, transactionid) AS prev_date
    FROM transactions_1k
)
SELECT 
    transactionid,
    date,
    time,
    customerid,
    cardid,
    gasstationid,
    productid,
    amount,
    price,
    CASE 
        WHEN prev_date IS NULL THEN 0
        ELSE GREATEST((date - prev_date), 0)
    END AS days_between
FROM OrderedTransactions

WITH consecutive_pairs AS (
    -- First, identify all consecutive purchase pairs
    SELECT 
        p1.product_name AS first_product,
        p2.product_name AS second_product
    FROM orders AS p1
    JOIN orders AS p2 
        ON p1.customer_id = p2.customer_id 
        AND p1.purchase_rank + 1 = p2.purchase_rank
),
pair_counts AS (
    -- Count occurrences of each specific pair
    SELECT 
        first_product,
        second_product,
        COUNT(*) AS pair_count
    FROM consecutive_pairs
    GROUP BY first_product, second_product
),
total_pairs AS (
    -- Calculate the grand total of all consecutive pairs
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS grand_total
    FROM consecutive_pairs
)
SELECT 
    pc.first_product,
    pc.second_product,
    pc.pair_count,
    ROUND(
        100.0 * pc.pair_count / NULLIF(tp.grand_total, 0), 
        4
    ) AS percentage_of_total
FROM pair_counts pc
CROSS JOIN total_pairs tp
ORDER BY pc.pair_count DESC

WITH all_ids AS (
    SELECT id FROM worker1 WHERE id IS NOT NULL AND id > 0
    UNION
    SELECT id FROM worker2 WHERE id IS NOT NULL AND id > 0
),
numbered AS (
    SELECT id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) AS rn
    FROM all_ids
),
gaps AS (
    SELECT 1 AS missing_value
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM all_ids WHERE id = 1)
    UNION ALL
    SELECT n.id + 1
    FROM numbered n
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM all_ids a WHERE a.id = n.id + 1
    )
    AND n.id + 1 > 0
)
SELECT MIN(missing_value) AS lowest_missing_value
FROM gaps

WITH event_budget_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_event,
        -- Check if any budget is Closed
        BOOL_OR(event_status = 'Closed') AS has_closed,
        -- Check if any budget is Open
        BOOL_OR(event_status = 'Open') AS has_open,
        -- Check if any budget is Planning
        BOOL_OR(event_status = 'Planning') AS has_planning,
        -- Max remaining for Closed entries
        MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN remaining END) AS max_remaining_closed,
        -- Max spent for Open entries
        MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN spent END) AS max_spent_open,
        -- Max spent for Planning entries
        MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN spent END) AS max_spent_planning
    FROM budget
    GROUP BY link_to_event
)
SELECT 
    link_to_event,
    -- Determine consolidated status based on priority rules
    CASE 
        WHEN has_closed THEN 'Closed'
        WHEN has_open AND has_planning THEN 'Open'
        WHEN has_open THEN 'Open'
        WHEN has_planning THEN 'Planning'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS consolidated_status,
    -- Calculate financial value based on consolidated status
    CASE 
        WHEN has_closed THEN max_remaining_closed
        WHEN has_open AND has_planning THEN COALESCE(max_spent_open, 0) + COALESCE(max_spent_planning, 0)
        WHEN has_open THEN max_spent_open
        WHEN has_planning THEN max_spent_planning
        ELSE NULL
    END AS financial_value
FROM event_budget_summary

WITH links AS (
    SELECT 
        greatest(link.source_cluster, link.target_cluster) AS big, 
        least(link.source_cluster, link.target_cluster) AS smol, 
        link.type AS type 
    FROM link AS link
) 
SELECT 
    l.cluster AS left_id, 
    l.cluster_type AS left_type, 
    l.cluster_label AS left_label, 
    r.cluster AS right_id, 
    r.cluster_type AS right_type, 
    r.cluster_label AS right_label, 
    count(distinct l.article) AS articles, 
    array_agg(distinct links.type) FILTER (WHERE links.type IS NOT NULL) AS link_types 
FROM tag AS l 
JOIN tag AS r ON l.article = r.article AND l.cluster > r.cluster 
LEFT JOIN links ON l.cluster = links.big AND r.cluster = links.smol 
GROUP BY 
    l.cluster, 
    l.cluster_label, 
    l.cluster_type, 
    r.cluster, 
    r.cluster_label, 
    r.cluster_type 
ORDER BY count(distinct l.article) DESC

WITH ordered AS (
    SELECT 
        alb,
        art,
        feat_arts,
        start_date,
        end_date,
        -- Create a flag for when a new group starts (gap > 1 day or first record)
        CASE 
            WHEN start_date != LAG(end_date) OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date) + INTERVAL '1 day'
            THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS is_new_group
    FROM sp_album_ctb
),
grouped AS (
    SELECT 
        alb,
        art,
        feat_arts,
        start_date,
        end_date,
        SUM(is_new_group) OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date) AS grp
    FROM ordered
)
SELECT 
    alb,
    art,
    feat_arts,
    MIN(start_date) AS start_date,
    CASE 
        WHEN BOOL_OR(end_date IS NULL) THEN NULL 
        ELSE MAX(end_date) 
    END AS end_date
FROM grouped
GROUP BY alb, art, feat_arts, grp
ORDER BY alb, art, feat_arts, start_date

    a2.element AS element2
FROM molecule AS m
INNER JOIN atom AS a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id

WITH molecule_pairs AS (
    SELECT 'TR427' AS mol_id1, 'TR430' AS mol_id2
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'TR427', 'TR431'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'TR430', 'TR431'
),
molecule_stats AS (
    SELECT
        m.molecule_id,
        m.label,
        COUNT(DISTINCT a.atom_id) AS atom_count,
        COUNT(DISTINCT b.bond_id) AS bond_count,
        STRING_AGG(DISTINCT a.element, ',' ORDER BY a.element) AS elements
    FROM molecule AS m
    LEFT JOIN atom AS a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
    LEFT JOIN bond AS b ON m.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
    GROUP BY m.molecule_id, m.label
)
SELECT
    m1.molecule_id AS molecule_id1,
    m1.label AS label1,
    m1.atom_count AS atom_count1,
    m1.elements AS elements1,
    m2.molecule_id AS molecule_id2,
    m2.label AS label2,
    m2.atom_count AS atom_count2,
    m2.elements AS elements2,
    CASE
        WHEN m1.bond_count > 0 AND m2.bond_count > 0 THEN 'Both have bonds'
        WHEN m1.bond_count > 0 THEN 'Only ' || m1.molecule_id || ' has bonds'
        WHEN m2.bond_count > 0 THEN 'Only ' || m2.molecule_id || ' has bonds'
        ELSE 'Neither has bonds'
    END AS bond_status
FROM molecule_pairs AS mp
JOIN molecule_stats AS m1 ON m1.molecule_id = mp.mol_id1
JOIN molecule_stats AS m2 ON m2.molecule_id = mp.mol_id2
ORDER BY m1.molecule_id, m2.molecule_id

WITH set_reprint_status AS (
    -- Determine which sets have reprints
    SELECT 
        setcode,
        MAX(isreprint) AS has_reprint
    FROM cards
    GROUP BY setcode
),
max_id_per_set AS (
    -- Find the maximum id for each set (for sets without reprints)
    SELECT 
        setcode,
        MAX(id) AS max_id
    FROM cards
    GROUP BY setcode
)
SELECT c.*
FROM cards c
JOIN set_reprint_status srs ON c.setcode = srs.setcode
LEFT JOIN max_id_per_set mips ON c.setcode = mips.setcode
WHERE 
    -- Condition 1: Set has reprints, include all cards
    srs.has_reprint = 1
    -- Condition 2: Set has no reprints, only include the max id card
    OR (srs.has_reprint = 0 AND c.id = mips.max_id)

WITH RECURSIVE tokenized_members AS (
    -- Base case: first token
    SELECT
        a.link_to_event,
        1 AS position,
        CASE 
            WHEN position('|' IN a.link_to_member) = 0 THEN a.link_to_member
            ELSE substring(a.link_to_member from 1 for position('|' IN a.link_to_member) - 1)
        END AS member_token,
        CASE 
            WHEN position('|' IN a.link_to_member) = 0 THEN ''
            ELSE substring(a.link_to_member from position('|' IN a.link_to_member) + 1)
        END AS remaining
    FROM attendance_trunc a
    WHERE a.link_to_member IS NOT NULL AND a.link_to_member <> ''
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: next token
    SELECT
        tm.link_to_event,
        tm.position + 1,
        CASE 
            WHEN position('|' IN tm.remaining) = 0 THEN tm.remaining
            ELSE substring(tm.remaining from 1 for position('|' IN tm.remaining) - 1)
        END AS member_token,
        CASE 
            WHEN position('|' IN tm.remaining) = 0 THEN ''
            ELSE substring(tm.remaining from position('|' IN tm.remaining) + 1)
        END AS remaining
    FROM tokenized_members tm
    WHERE tm.remaining <> ''
),
member_names AS (
    SELECT
        tm.link_to_event,
        tm.position,
        COALESCE(mt.member_name, 'Unknown Member') AS member_name
    FROM tokenized_members tm
    LEFT JOIN member_trunc mt ON tm.member_token = mt.member_id
)
SELECT
    mn.link_to_event,
    string_agg(mn.member_name, '|' ORDER BY mn.position) AS replaced_members
FROM member_names mn
GROUP BY mn.link_to_event
ORDER BY mn.link_to_event

WITH zero_consumption AS (
    -- Filter to zero consumption records and parse the date
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        date,
        -- Convert YYYYMM to a proper integer for calculations: YYYY * 12 + MM
        (CAST(SUBSTRING(date, 1, 4) AS INTEGER) * 12 + CAST(SUBSTRING(date, 5, 2) AS INTEGER)) AS month_seq
    FROM yearmonth
    WHERE consumption = 0
),
ranked AS (
    -- Add row number partitioned by customer and ordered by month
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        date,
        month_seq,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid ORDER BY month_seq) AS rn
    FROM zero_consumption
),
grouped AS (
    -- Create group identifier: consecutive months have same (month_seq - rn)
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        date,
        month_seq,
        (month_seq - rn) AS grp
    FROM ranked
)
-- Get the start and end of each continuous period
SELECT 
    customerid,
    MIN(date) AS start_month,
    MAX(date) AS end_month,
    COUNT(*) AS months_in_period
FROM grouped
GROUP BY customerid, grp
ORDER BY customerid, start_month

WITH time_points AS (
    SELECT start AS ts FROM duration WHERE start IS NOT NULL
    UNION
    SELECT "end" AS ts FROM duration WHERE "end" IS NOT NULL
),
ordered_points AS (
    SELECT ts,
           LEAD(ts) OVER (ORDER BY ts) AS next_ts
    FROM time_points
),
intervals AS (
    SELECT ts AS interval_start,
           next_ts AS interval_end
    FROM ordered_points
    WHERE next_ts IS NOT NULL
),
concurrency_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        i.interval_start,
        i.interval_end,
        COUNT(d.id) AS concurrent_events
    FROM intervals i
    JOIN duration d 
        ON d.start < i.interval_end 
        AND d."end" > i.interval_start
    GROUP BY i.interval_start, i.interval_end
)
SELECT MAX(concurrent_events) AS max_concurrent_events
FROM concurrency_counts

WITH TeamSequences AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        team_api_id,
        date,
        buildupplayspeed,
        buildupplaypassing,
        chancecreationshooting,
        defenceteamwidth,
        -- Look forward: next record's values for the same team
        LEAD(buildupplayspeed) OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY date) AS next_buildupplayspeed,
        LEAD(buildupplaypassing) OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY date) AS next_buildupplaypassing,
        -- Look backward: previous record's values for the same team
        LAG(defenceteamwidth) OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY date) AS prev_defenceteamwidth,
        LAG(chancecreationshooting) OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY date) AS prev_chancecreationshooting,
        -- Count total records for this team to filter later
        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id) AS team_record_count
    FROM team_attributes
)
SELECT
    id,
    team_api_id,
    date,
    buildupplayspeed,
    buildupplaypassing,
    next_buildupplayspeed,
    next_buildupplaypassing,
    defenceteamwidth,
    chancecreationshooting,
    prev_defenceteamwidth,
    prev_chancecreationshooting
FROM TeamSequences
WHERE 
    -- Only teams with more than one record
    team_record_count > 1
    -- Previous defensive width > current build-up speed
    AND prev_defenceteamwidth > buildupplayspeed
ORDER BY team_api_id, date

  FROM "match"

t2 AS (
  SELECT 
    t."date",
    t.code,
    t.duration,
    t.match_date_only,

      PARTITION BY t.match_date_only, t.code, t.duration

    MIN(t.duration) OVER (
      PARTITION BY t.match_date_only, t.code
    ) AS min_dur
  FROM t
)

  t2."date",
  t2.code,
  t2.duration,

MERGE INTO treatment_and_followup_latest AS target
USING treatment_and_followup AS source
ON target.patient_id = source.patient_id
WHEN MATCHED THEN
    UPDATE SET 
        treatment_followup_id = source.treatment_followup_id,
        followup_no = source.followup_no,
        primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score = source.primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score,

        day_of_form_completion = source.day_of_form_completion,

WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN

            source.project, source.day_of_form_completion, source.month_of_form_completion,

WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE THEN

    SELECT 4, 20 UNION ALL 
    SELECT 5, 55 UNION ALL 
    SELECT 5, 1 UNION ALL 
    SELECT 6, 1 UNION ALL 
    SELECT 6, 13
), 
items AS (
    SELECT 10 AS hero_id, 1 AS power_id UNION ALL

page_power_counts AS (

    FROM pages

hero_page_matches AS (

    FROM "match"
),
streak_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        "team_api_id",
        match_date,
        is_win,
        SUM(CASE WHEN is_win = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (
            PARTITION BY "team_api_id" 
            ORDER BY match_date
            ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING
        ) AS streak_group
    FROM home_matches
),
winning_streaks AS (
    SELECT

        MAX(match_date) AS "end_date",

)

ORDER BY "team_api_id", "start_date"

WITH PatientExamData AS (
    SELECT 
        P.ID AS PATIENTID, 
        P.SEX, 
        P.BIRTHDAY, 
        E."Examination Date", 
        E.DIAGNOSIS, 
        E.THROMBOSIS, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY P.ID ORDER BY E."Examination Date" NULLS FIRST) AS ExamRank 
    FROM patient AS P 
    LEFT JOIN examination AS E ON P.ID = E.ID
), 
LatestLabResults AS (
    SELECT 
        L.ID, 
        L.PLT, 
        L.WBC, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY L.ID ORDER BY L."date" DESC NULLS LAST) AS LabRank 
    FROM laboratory AS L 
    WHERE L."date" <= CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
), 
PatientRiskProfile AS (
    SELECT 
        PED.PATIENTID, 
        PED.SEX, 
        PED.BIRTHDAY, 
        PED."Examination Date", 
        PED.DIAGNOSIS, 
        PED.THROMBOSIS, 
        CASE 
            WHEN LLR.PLT < 150 OR LLR.WBC > 11 THEN 'High' 
            WHEN PED.THROMBOSIS IS NULL THEN 'Unknown' 
            ELSE 'Normal' 
        END AS RiskCategory 
    FROM PatientExamData AS PED 
    LEFT JOIN LatestLabResults AS LLR 
        ON PED.PATIENTID = LLR.ID 
        AND LLR.LabRank = 1
) 
SELECT 
    PATIENTID, 
    SEX, 
    BIRTHDAY, 
    COUNT(*) AS EXAMINATIONCOUNT, 
    SUM(CASE WHEN THROMBOSIS = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS THROMBOSISCOUNT, 
    SUM(CASE WHEN THROMBOSIS = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS NOTHROMBOSISCOUNT, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN RiskCategory = 'High' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS HighRiskFlag 
FROM PatientRiskProfile 
GROUP BY PATIENTID, SEX, BIRTHDAY 
HAVING COUNT(*) > 0 
ORDER BY 
    SUM(CASE WHEN THROMBOSIS = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) DESC NULLS LAST, 
    PATIENTID NULLS FIRST

DriverFirstFiveRaces AS (

WITH t AS

          t."Examination Date" AS startdate,
          t.seqnum,

          t."Examination Date" AS anchordate
   FROM t
   WHERE t.seqnum = 1
   UNION ALL 
   SELECT t.id,
          t."Examination Date" AS startdate,
          t.seqnum,
          CASE
              WHEN t."Examination Date" > (cte.anchordate + INTERVAL '1 year') THEN 'Y'
              ELSE 'N'
          END AS isrehire,
          CASE
              WHEN t."Examination Date" > (cte.anchordate + INTERVAL '1 year') THEN t."Examination Date"
              ELSE cte.anchordate
          END AS anchordate
   FROM cte
   INNER JOIN t ON cte.id = t.id AND cte.seqnum + 1 = t.seqnum)

       startdate,

       isrehire

         startdate

WITH RECURSIVE set_hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: start with sets that have a parent
    SELECT 
        id,
        code,
        parentcode,
        code::text AS path,
        1 AS level
    FROM sets
    WHERE parentcode IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: join with parent sets to extend the path
    SELECT 
        sh.id,
        sh.code,
        s.parentcode,
        s.code || '-' || sh.path AS path,
        sh.level + 1 AS level
    FROM set_hierarchy sh
    JOIN sets s ON sh.parentcode = s.code
    WHERE s.parentcode IS NOT NULL OR s.code IS NOT NULL
),
-- Get the maximum level (deepest path) for each set
max_levels AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        code,
        path,
        level,
        MAX(level) OVER (PARTITION BY id) AS max_level
    FROM set_hierarchy
)
SELECT 
    id,
    code,
    path AS ancestralCode
FROM max_levels
WHERE level = max_level
ORDER BY id

                      '2023-01-02',
                      20,
                      1), (3,
                           '2023-02-01',

                                '2023-02-02',
                                25,
                                1), (5,
                                     '2023-01-03',
                                     30,
                                     2), (6,
                                          '2023-02-03',
                                          40,
                                          2)) AS t (id, activity_date, activity_value, hero_id)),
     activity_with_months AS
  (SELECT id,
          activity_date,
          activity_value,
          DATE_TRUNC('month', activity_date) AS month_start,
          SUM(activity_value) OVER (
                                    ORDER BY activity_date) AS run_tot,

   FROM hero_activity),
     monthly_sums AS

   FROM hero_activity),
     monthly_with_lag AS

          month_total,
          LAG(month_total) OVER (ORDER BY month_start) AS prev_month_total
   FROM monthly_sums)
SELECT a.id,
       a.activity_date,
       a.activity_value,
       a.run_tot,
       a.m_run_tot,

WITH match_dates AS (
    SELECT m.country_id,
           m.date,
           m.league_id,
           m.season,
           DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY m.country_id ORDER BY m.date DESC) AS date_rank
    FROM "match" m
    WHERE m.date IS NOT NULL
),
ranked_matches AS (
    SELECT md.country_id,
           md.date,
           md.date_rank,
           l.name AS league_name
    FROM match_dates md
    JOIN "league" l ON md.league_id = l.id
    WHERE md.date_rank <= 2
),
pivoted_dates AS (
    SELECT country_id,
           date_rank,
           MAX(date) AS match_date,
           string_agg(DISTINCT league_name, ', ' ORDER BY league_name) AS leagues_played
    FROM ranked_matches
    GROUP BY country_id, date_rank
),
country_stats AS (
    SELECT c.id AS country_id,
           c.name AS country_name,
           COUNT(DISTINCT m.league_id) AS league_count,
           COUNT(DISTINCT m.season) AS season_count
    FROM "country" c
    LEFT JOIN "match" m ON c.id = m.country_id
    GROUP BY c.id, c.name
)
SELECT cs.country_id,
       cs.country_name,
       MAX(CASE WHEN pd.date_rank = 1 THEN pd.match_date END) AS last_match_date,
       MAX(CASE WHEN pd.date_rank = 1 THEN pd.leagues_played END) AS last_match_leagues,
       MAX(CASE WHEN pd.date_rank = 2 THEN pd.match_date END) AS prev_match_date,
       MAX(CASE WHEN pd.date_rank = 2 THEN pd.leagues_played END) AS prev_match_leagues,
       cs.league_count,
       cs.season_count
FROM country_stats cs
LEFT JOIN pivoted_dates pd ON cs.country_id = pd.country_id
GROUP BY cs.country_id, cs.country_name, cs.league_count, cs.season_count
HAVING MAX(CASE WHEN pd.date_rank = 1 THEN pd.match_date END) IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY last_match_date DESC

WithPrevious AS (

SchoolChanges AS (

FundingChanges AS (

      AND rn > 1

WITH monthly_last_exam AS (
    -- Get the last examination for each patient in each month
    SELECT 
        id,
        "Examination Date",
        thrombosis,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', "Examination Date")::DATE AS month_start,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY id, DATE_TRUNC('month', "Examination Date")
            ORDER BY "Examination Date" DESC
        ) AS rn
    FROM examination
),
filtered_last_exams AS (
    -- Keep only the last exam of each month
    SELECT 
        id,
        "Examination Date",
        thrombosis,
        month_start
    FROM monthly_last_exam
    WHERE rn = 1
),
with_previous AS (
    -- Get the previous examination's thrombosis value (from any prior date)
    SELECT 
        id,
        month_start,
        "Examination Date",
        thrombosis,
        LAG(thrombosis) OVER (
            PARTITION BY id 
            ORDER BY "Examination Date"
        ) AS prev_thrombosis
    FROM filtered_last_exams
)
SELECT 
    id,
    month_start AS startofmonth,
    CASE 
        WHEN prev_thrombosis IS NULL THEN 'No'  -- No previous exam to compare
        WHEN prev_thrombosis < thrombosis THEN 'Yes'
        ELSE 'No'
    END AS increase_risk,
    CASE 
        WHEN prev_thrombosis IS NULL THEN 'No'  -- No previous exam to compare
        WHEN prev_thrombosis > thrombosis THEN 'Yes'
        ELSE 'No'
    END AS decrease_risk
FROM with_previous
ORDER BY id, month_start

WITH RECURSIVE ExpOrdered AS (
    -- Get all experiments using material 2, ordered by exp_id
    SELECT 
        exp_id,
        mid,
        qty,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY exp_id) AS rn
    FROM experiment
    WHERE mid = 2
),
RunningInventory AS (
    -- Base case: first experiment
    SELECT 
        e.exp_id,
        e.mid,
        e.qty,
        i.qty AS initial_stock,
        CASE 
            WHEN e.qty <= i.qty THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS executable,
        CASE 
            WHEN e.qty <= i.qty THEN i.qty - e.qty 
            ELSE i.qty 
        END AS remaining_stock
    FROM ExpOrdered e
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT qty FROM inventory WHERE mid = 2) i
    WHERE e.rn = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent experiments
    SELECT 
        e.exp_id,
        e.mid,
        e.qty,
        r.remaining_stock AS initial_stock,
        CASE 
            WHEN e.qty <= r.remaining_stock THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS executable,
        CASE 
            WHEN e.qty <= r.remaining_stock THEN r.remaining_stock - e.qty 
            ELSE r.remaining_stock 
        END AS remaining_stock
    FROM ExpOrdered e
    INNER JOIN RunningInventory r ON e.rn = r.rn + 1
)
SELECT 
    exp_id, 
    mid, 
    executable AS status
FROM RunningInventory
ORDER BY exp_id

WITH PaymentRanks AS (

DistinctPaymentCount AS (

),
TotalCount AS (

    FROM loan

SELECT

    END AS SecondHighest

WITH

    set_a (molecule_id, element_count) AS (
        SELECT molecule_id, COUNT(DISTINCT element)
        FROM atom
        GROUP BY molecule_id
        HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT element) > 1
    ),
    set_b (molecule_id, element_count) AS (
        SELECT m.molecule_id, COUNT(DISTINCT a.element)
        FROM molecule m
        JOIN atom a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
        WHERE m.label IS NOT NULL
        GROUP BY m.molecule_id
    ),

    unique_a (molecule_id, element_count, rn, density_rank) AS (
        SELECT
            molecule_id,
            element_count,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY element_count) AS rn,
            DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY element_count DESC) AS density_rank
        FROM set_a
    ),
    unique_b (molecule_id, element_count, rn, density_rank) AS (
        SELECT
            molecule_id,
            element_count,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY element_count) AS rn,
            DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY element_count DESC) AS density_rank
        FROM set_b
    ),

    -- Fixed: Proper recursive CTE with termination condition
    molecule_hierarchy AS (
        SELECT
            molecule_id,
            molecule_id AS root_molecule,
            1 AS hierarchy_level
        FROM molecule
        WHERE label = '+'

        UNION ALL

        SELECT
            m.molecule_id,
            h.root_molecule,
            h.hierarchy_level + 1
        FROM molecule m
        JOIN molecule_hierarchy h ON m.molecule_id = h.root_molecule
        WHERE h.hierarchy_level < 10  -- Prevent infinite recursion
    ),

    -- Pre-filter set_a to only include molecules with the specific bond
    filtered_a AS (
        SELECT a.*
        FROM unique_a a
        WHERE EXISTS (
            SELECT 1
            FROM bond b
            WHERE b.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
            AND b.bond_id = 'TR011_2_24'
        )
        AND EXISTS (
            SELECT 1
            FROM connected c
            JOIN atom a1 ON c.atom_id = a1.atom_id
            JOIN atom a2 ON c.atom_id2 = a2.atom_id
            WHERE a1.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
            AND a1.element = a2.element
        )
    ),

    -- Calculate average for HAVING clause
    set_a_stats AS (
        SELECT AVG(element_count) AS avg_element_count
        FROM set_a
    )

SELECT
    COUNT(CASE WHEN a.rn = b.rn AND a.density_rank = b.density_rank THEN 1 END) AS num_identical_in_a_and_b,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN a.molecule_id IS NOT NULL AND b.molecule_id IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS num_not_identical_in_a,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN a.molecule_id IS NULL AND b.molecule_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS num_not_identical_in_b,
    STRING_AGG(COALESCE(a.molecule_id, b.molecule_id), ',') WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY COALESCE(a.density_rank, b.density_rank)) AS matching_molecules,
    JSON_AGG(
        JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
            'molecule', COALESCE(a.molecule_id, b.molecule_id),
            'rank', COALESCE(a.density_rank, b.density_rank)
        )
        ORDER BY COALESCE(a.density_rank, b.density_rank)
    ) AS molecule_json
FROM filtered_a a
FULL OUTER JOIN unique_b b ON a.molecule_id = b.molecule_id AND a.rn = b.rn
LEFT JOIN molecule_hierarchy h ON COALESCE(a.molecule_id, b.molecule_id) = h.molecule_id
CROSS JOIN set_a_stats
GROUP BY
    GROUPING SETS (
        (a.density_rank, b.density_rank),
        ()
    )
HAVING
    COUNT(DISTINCT COALESCE(a.molecule_id, b.molecule_id)) > MAX(set_a_stats.avg_element_count)

WITH KEYWORDMATCHES AS
  (SELECT P.ID,
          CASE
              WHEN POSITION(LOWER('Aaron') IN LOWER(P.PLAYER_NAME)) > 0 THEN 'Aaron'
          END AS COLAKEYWORD,
          CASE
              WHEN POSITION(LOWER('Cresswell') IN LOWER(P.PLAYER_NAME)) > 0 THEN 'Cresswell'
          END AS COLBKEYWORD,
          CASE
              WHEN POSITION(LOWER('Doran') IN LOWER(P.PLAYER_NAME)) > 0 THEN 'Doran'
          END AS COLCKEYWORD
   FROM PLAYER AS P),
     KEYWORDSCORES AS
  (SELECT ID,
          STRING_AGG(COLAKEYWORD, ', ') FILTER (WHERE COLAKEYWORD IS NOT NULL) AS KEYWORDSA,
          STRING_AGG(COLBKEYWORD, ', ') FILTER (WHERE COLBKEYWORD IS NOT NULL) AS KEYWORDSB,
          STRING_AGG(COLCKEYWORD, ', ') FILTER (WHERE COLCKEYWORD IS NOT NULL) AS KEYWORDSC,
          SUM(CASE
                  WHEN COLAKEYWORD IS NOT NULL THEN 10
                  ELSE 0
              END) AS COLASCORE,
          SUM(CASE
                  WHEN COLBKEYWORD IS NOT NULL THEN 5
                  ELSE 0
              END) AS COLBSCORE,
          SUM(CASE
                  WHEN COLCKEYWORD IS NOT NULL THEN 0.1
                  ELSE 0
              END) AS COLCSCORE
   FROM KEYWORDMATCHES AS KM
   GROUP BY ID)
SELECT DISTINCT P.ID,
                P.PLAYER_NAME AS DESCRIPTION,
                (KS.COLASCORE + KS.COLBSCORE + KS.COLCSCORE) AS SCORE
FROM PLAYER AS P
INNER JOIN KEYWORDSCORES AS KS ON P.ID = KS.ID
ORDER BY (KS.COLASCORE + KS.COLBSCORE + KS.COLCSCORE) DESC

WITH my_table AS (
    SELECT 'A' AS cto, 1 AS nif, 'A1' AS file_, 5 AS filesize
    UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 1, 'A2', 3
    UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 2, 'A3', 2
    UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 2, 'A4', 1
    UNION ALL SELECT 'B', 1, 'B1', 4
    UNION ALL SELECT 'B', 1, 'B2', 6
),
-- Add row numbers and group identifiers by nif within each cto
numbered AS (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY cto ORDER BY file_) AS rn,
           SUM(filesize) OVER (PARTITION BY cto, nif) AS nif_total_size,
           MIN(rn) OVER (PARTITION BY cto, nif) AS nif_first_rn,
           MAX(rn) OVER (PARTITION BY cto, nif) AS nif_last_rn
    FROM (
        SELECT *,
               ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY cto ORDER BY file_) AS rn
        FROM my_table
    ) t
),
-- Get distinct nif groups with their aggregate info
nif_groups AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT cto, nif, nif_total_size, nif_first_rn, nif_last_rn,
           DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY cto ORDER BY nif_first_rn) AS nif_group_num
    FROM numbered
),
-- Recursive CTE to assign partitions based on lookahead logic
partition_assignment AS (
    -- Base case: first nif group in each category
    SELECT cto, nif_group_num, nif, nif_total_size, nif_first_rn, nif_last_rn,
           1 AS partition_num,
           nif_total_size AS current_partition_size,
           1 AS items_in_partition
    FROM nif_groups
    WHERE nif_group_num = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: add next nif group if it fits, else start new partition
    SELECT ng.cto, ng.nif_group_num, ng.nif, ng.nif_total_size, ng.nif_first_rn, ng.nif_last_rn,
           CASE 
               -- Start new partition if: would exceed size 10 OR already have 7 items
               WHEN pa.current_partition_size + ng.nif_total_size > 10 
                    OR pa.items_in_partition >= 7 
               THEN pa.partition_num + 1
               ELSE pa.partition_num
           END AS partition_num,
           CASE 
               WHEN pa.current_partition_size + ng.nif_total_size > 10 
                    OR pa.items_in_partition >= 7 
               THEN ng.nif_total_size
               ELSE pa.current_partition_size + ng.nif_total_size
           END AS current_partition_size,
           CASE 
               WHEN pa.current_partition_size + ng.nif_total_size > 10 
                    OR pa.items_in_partition >= 7 
               THEN 1
               ELSE pa.items_in_partition + 1
           END AS items_in_partition
    FROM nif_groups ng
    JOIN partition_assignment pa 
        ON ng.cto = pa.cto AND ng.nif_group_num = pa.nif_group_num + 1
),
-- Calculate final partition sizes
partition_sizes AS (
    SELECT cto, partition_num, SUM(nif_total_size) AS total_size
    FROM partition_assignment
    GROUP BY cto, partition_num
),
-- Assign subpartition numbers (position within partition)
final_partitions AS (
    SELECT pa.cto, pa.nif, pa.nif_first_rn, pa.nif_last_rn, 
           pa.partition_num,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY pa.cto, pa.partition_num 
                              ORDER BY pa.nif_group_num) AS subpartition,
           ps.total_size
    FROM partition_assignment pa
    JOIN partition_sizes ps 
        ON pa.cto = ps.cto AND pa.partition_num = ps.partition_num
)
-- Join back to original rows
SELECT n.cto,
       n.nif,
       n.file_,
       fp.partition_num AS partition,
       fp.subpartition,
       fp.total_size
FROM numbered n
JOIN final_partitions fp 
    ON n.cto = fp.cto 
    AND n.nif = fp.nif 
    AND n.rn BETWEEN fp.nif_first_rn AND fp.nif_last_rn
ORDER BY n.cto, n.rn

WITH date_ranges AS (
    -- Define the input date ranges
    SELECT TO_DATE('2014-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_from,
           TO_DATE('2014-01-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_until
    FROM schools
    UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE('2014-02-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_from,
           TO_DATE('2014-02-28', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_until
    FROM schools
),
events AS (
    -- Create +1 event at start, -1 event at day after end (half-open intervals)
    SELECT valid_from AS dt, 1 AS delta
    FROM date_ranges
    UNION ALL
    SELECT valid_until + INTERVAL '1 day' AS dt, -1 AS delta
    FROM date_ranges
),
cumulative AS (
    -- Calculate running count at each event point
    SELECT dt,
           SUM(delta) OVER (ORDER BY dt) AS count
    FROM events
),
periods AS (
    -- Create periods between consecutive events
    SELECT dt AS valid_from,
           LEAD(dt) OVER (ORDER BY dt) - INTERVAL '1 day' AS valid_until,
           count
    FROM cumulative
)
SELECT valid_from,
       valid_until,
       count
FROM periods
WHERE count > 0
  AND (valid_from <= valid_until OR valid_until IS NULL)

WITH RECURSIVE vt as (
    select t.*, row_number() over (partition by CustomerID order by StartDate, TransactionID) as seqnum
    from transactions_1k t
),
cte as (
    -- Base case: first transaction for each customer is always unflagged
    select 
        vt.TransactionID, 
        vt.CustomerID, 
        vt.StartDate, 
        vt.EndDate, 
        vt.seqnum,
        0 as flag,
        vt.EndDate as last_unflagged_end_date
    from vt
    where seqnum = 1
    
    union all
    
    -- Recursive case: compare current transaction against last unflagged end date
    select 
        vt.TransactionID, 
        vt.CustomerID, 
        vt.StartDate, 
        vt.EndDate,
        vt.seqnum,
        case 
            when vt.StartDate <= cte.last_unflagged_end_date + interval '90 days' then 1
            else 0
        end as flag,
        case 
            when vt.StartDate <= cte.last_unflagged_end_date + interval '90 days' then cte.last_unflagged_end_date
            else vt.EndDate
        end as last_unflagged_end_date
    from cte 
    join vt on vt.seqnum = cte.seqnum + 1 and vt.CustomerID = cte.CustomerID
)
select 
    cte.TransactionID, 
    cte.CustomerID, 
    cte.StartDate, 
    cte.EndDate,
    cte.flag
from cte
order by cte.CustomerID, cte.StartDate, cte.TransactionID

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
    -- Anchor: start with the given IDs, track the original starting ID
    SELECT 
        ID, 
        ParentID,
        ID as original_id  -- preserve the starting point
    FROM LaboratoryLink
    WHERE ID IN (657266, 115562)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive: follow parent links upward
    SELECT 
        t.ID, 
        t.ParentID,
        c.original_id  -- carry forward the original starting ID
    FROM LaboratoryLink t
    JOIN cte c ON t.ID = c.ParentID
    WHERE c.ParentID IS NOT NULL  -- stop when we can't go further up
)
-- Select only the root records (where ParentID IS NULL)
-- and show which original starting ID they belong to
SELECT 
    original_id as starting_id,
    ID as ultimate_root_id
FROM cte
WHERE ParentID IS NULL

WITH weekly_cards AS (

        -- Calculate week number starting from Jan 1, 2007 (week 1 = Jan 1-7, 2007)
        FLOOR((to_date(originalreleasedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD') - DATE '2007-01-01') / 7)::int + 1 AS week_number
    FROM cards 
    WHERE originalreleasedate IS NOT NULL 
      AND to_date(originalreleasedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD') >= DATE '2007-01-01'
)
SELECT 
    (DATE '2007-01-01' + (week_number - 1) * INTERVAL '1 week')::date AS week_start_date,
    week_number,
    COUNT(*) AS card_count
FROM weekly_cards
GROUP BY week_number
ORDER BY week_start_date

    MAX(enddate) AS enddate

    SELECT *,
        SUM(CASE WHEN startdate <= prev_enddate THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY driverid, contracttype ORDER BY startdate) AS grp
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            driverid, 
            contracttype, 
            startdate, 
            enddate,
            LAG(enddate) OVER (PARTITION BY driverid, contracttype ORDER BY startdate) AS prev_enddate
        FROM drivercontracts
    ) a
) b
GROUP BY driverid, contracttype, grp
ORDER BY driverid, contracttype, startdate

WITH RECURSIVE
-- First, merge all overlapping blackout periods into continuous ranges
merged_blackouts AS (
    -- Start with the earliest blackout period that doesn't overlap with any earlier one
    SELECT start_date, end_date, 1 as grp
    FROM blackout_periods b1
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM blackout_periods b2
        WHERE b2.start_date < b1.start_date 
        AND b1.start_date <= b2.end_date
    )
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursively extend with overlapping periods
    SELECT 
        m.start_date,
        GREATEST(m.end_date, b.end_date),
        m.grp
    FROM merged_blackouts m
    JOIN blackout_periods b 
        ON b.start_date <= m.end_date 
        AND b.end_date > m.end_date
),
-- Get the final merged blackout ranges (take max end_date for each group)
final_blackouts AS (
    SELECT start_date, MAX(end_date) as end_date
    FROM merged_blackouts
    GROUP BY start_date
),
-- Add sentinel values for boundaries and create clear periods (gaps between blackouts)
clear_periods AS (
    -- Period before first blackout
    SELECT 
        '1900-01-01'::date as start_date,
        (MIN(start_date) - INTERVAL '1 day')::date as end_date
    FROM final_blackouts
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Gaps between consecutive blackouts
    SELECT 
        (end_date + INTERVAL '1 day')::date as start_date,
        (LEAD(start_date) OVER (ORDER BY start_date) - INTERVAL '1 day')::date as end_date
    FROM final_blackouts
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Period after last blackout
    SELECT 
        (MAX(end_date) + INTERVAL '1 day')::date as start_date,
        '9999-12-31'::date as end_date
    FROM final_blackouts
),
-- Filter out null end dates (last gap) and invalid ranges
valid_clear_periods AS (
    SELECT start_date, end_date
    FROM clear_periods
    WHERE end_date IS NOT NULL 
    AND start_date <= end_date
)
-- Final result: intersection of active periods and clear periods
SELECT 
    GREATEST(a.start_date, c.start_date) as start_date,
    LEAST(a.end_date, c.end_date) as end_date
FROM account_activity a
INNER JOIN valid_clear_periods c
    ON a.start_date <= c.end_date 
    AND c.start_date <= a.end_date
WHERE GREATEST(a.start_date, c.start_date) <= LEAST(a.end_date, c.end_date)
ORDER BY start_date
