SELECT 
    ct.c_id, 
    bt.start_time::date AS trip_date, 
    trip_counts.trip_count,
    CASE 
        WHEN trip_counts.trip_count = 1 THEN single.cost
        WHEN trip_counts.trip_count = 2 THEN single.cost * 2
        ELSE day.cost
    END AS total_cost
FROM customertrip ct
JOIN bustrip bt ON ct.b_id = bt.b_id
JOIN (
    SELECT 
        c_id, 
        bt_inner.start_time::date AS trip_date, 
        COUNT(*) AS trip_count
    FROM customertrip ct_inner
    JOIN bustrip bt_inner ON ct_inner.b_id = bt_inner.b_id
    GROUP BY c_id, bt_inner.start_time::date
) trip_counts ON ct.c_id = trip_counts.c_id 
    AND bt.start_time::date = trip_counts.trip_date
CROSS JOIN (SELECT cost FROM ticketcosts WHERE duration = 'Single') AS single
CROSS JOIN (SELECT cost FROM ticketcosts WHERE duration = 'Day') AS day
GROUP BY ct.c_id, bt.start_time::date, trip_counts.trip_count, single.cost, day.cost

WITH attendance_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        COUNT(*) AS attendance_count
    FROM attendance
    GROUP BY link_to_member, link_to_event
),
member_event_rank AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_member ORDER BY attendance_count DESC, link_to_event) AS event_rank
    FROM attendance_stats
),
event_member_rank AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_event ORDER BY attendance_count DESC, link_to_member) AS member_rank
    FROM attendance_stats
)
SELECT 
    mer.link_to_member,
    mer.link_to_event,
    mer.attendance_count,
    mer.event_rank AS "rank_of_event_for_member",
    emr.member_rank AS "rank_of_member_for_event"
FROM member_event_rank mer
JOIN event_member_rank emr 
    ON mer.link_to_member = emr.link_to_member 
    AND mer.link_to_event = emr.link_to_event
WHERE mer.event_rank <= 2      -- top 2 events per member
   OR emr.member_rank = 1      -- OR top 1 member per event
ORDER BY 
    mer.link_to_member,
    mer.event_rank

WITH string1_tab AS (
    SELECT regexp_substr('24,36,78,98', '[^,]+', 1, generate_series) AS value
    FROM generate_series(1, LENGTH('24,36,78,98') - LENGTH(REPLACE('24,36,78,98', ',', '')) + 1) AS generate_series
),
string2_tab AS (
    SELECT regexp_substr('25,36,88,98', '[^,]+', 1, generate_series) AS value
    FROM generate_series(1, LENGTH('25,36,88,98') - LENGTH(REPLACE('25,36,88,98', ',', '')) + 1) AS generate_series
),
common_values AS (
    SELECT TRIM(s1.value) AS value
    FROM string1_tab s1
    JOIN string2_tab s2 ON TRIM(s1.value) = TRIM(s2.value)
)
SELECT STRING_AGG(value, ',' ORDER BY value::int) AS common_values
FROM common_values
WHERE value IS NOT NULL

WITH event_years AS (
    SELECT 
        type,
        EXTRACT(YEAR FROM event_date::timestamp) AS year_num
    FROM event
    WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL
),
type_year_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        type,
        year_num,
        COUNT(*) AS year_count
    FROM event_years
    GROUP BY type, year_num
),
type_year_ranges AS (
    SELECT 
        type,
        MIN(year_num) AS earliest_year,
        MAX(year_num) AS latest_year
    FROM event_years
    GROUP BY type
)
SELECT 
    t.type AS event_type,
    COALESCE(latest.year_count, 0) - COALESCE(earliest.year_count, 0) AS year_difference
FROM type_year_ranges t
LEFT JOIN type_year_counts earliest 
    ON t.type = earliest.type AND t.earliest_year = earliest.year_num
LEFT JOIN type_year_counts latest 
    ON t.type = latest.type AND t.latest_year = latest.year_num

        WHEN bond_type = '#' THEN 'triple'

    END AS val
FROM bond

WITH tiers AS (
    -- Define the tier structure: start_qty, end_qty, rate
    SELECT 1 AS start_qty, 4 AS end_qty, 20 AS rate
    UNION ALL SELECT 5, 8, 50
    UNION ALL SELECT 10, 12, 100
),
input AS (
    -- The input quantity (can be parameterized)
    SELECT 6 AS input_qty
),
tiered_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        t.start_qty,
        t.end_qty,
        t.rate,
        -- Calculate how many units from this tier are used
        GREATEST(0, LEAST(t.end_qty, i.input_qty) - t.start_qty + 1) AS units_in_tier,
        -- Calculate the value for this tier
        GREATEST(0, LEAST(t.end_qty, i.input_qty) - t.start_qty + 1) * t.rate AS tier_value
    FROM tiers t
    CROSS JOIN input i
    WHERE t.start_qty <= i.input_qty  -- Only include tiers that overlap with input
)
SELECT 
    SUM(tier_value) AS total_value,
    SUM(units_in_tier) AS total_units_accounted
FROM tiered_calculation

WITH src(id, geom) AS (
    VALUES 
        (1, 'linestring(0 0, 0 10)'::geometry), 
        (2, 'linestring(0 10, 0 11)'::geometry), 
        (3, 'linestring(1 1, 0 5)'::geometry), 
        (4, 'linestring(1 1, 2 2)'::geometry)
)
SELECT DISTINCT a.id, a.geom
FROM src AS a
JOIN src AS b ON a.id != b.id AND ST_Intersects(a.geom, b.geom)
WHERE 
    -- The lines intersect at a point (not just touch at endpoints)
    ST_GeometryType(ST_Intersection(a.geom, b.geom)) = 'ST_Point'
    -- AND the intersection point is NOT at either endpoint of line a
    AND NOT ST_Equals(ST_Intersection(a.geom, b.geom), ST_StartPoint(a.geom))
    AND NOT ST_Equals(ST_Intersection(a.geom, b.geom), ST_EndPoint(a.geom))

WITH RECURSIVE date_range AS (
    -- Get the min and max dates from the filtered data
    SELECT 
        DATE '2024-10-01' AS min_date,
        DATE '2024-10-09' AS max_date
),
all_days AS (
    -- Generate all days in the range
    SELECT generate_series(
        (SELECT min_date FROM date_range),
        (SELECT max_date FROM date_range),
        INTERVAL '1 day'
    )::date AS day_date
),
working_days AS (
    -- Filter for weekdays only (Monday=1 to Friday=5)
    SELECT day_date 
    FROM all_days 
    WHERE EXTRACT(DOW FROM day_date) BETWEEN 1 AND 5
),
event_day_segments AS (
    -- For each event, generate segments for each working day it spans
    SELECT 
        e.learner_code,
        e.resource_key,
        e.start_timestamp,
        e.end_timestamp,
        wd.day_date,
        -- Calculate the start of this day segment (max of event start and 9 AM of this day)
        GREATEST(e.start_timestamp, wd.day_date + TIME '09:00:00') AS segment_start,
        -- Calculate the end of this day segment (min of event end and 6 PM of this day)
        LEAST(e.end_timestamp, wd.day_date + TIME '18:00:00') AS segment_end
    FROM event_usage e
    CROSS JOIN working_days wd
    WHERE e.start_timestamp BETWEEN '2024-10-01' AND '2024-10-09'
      -- Only include days where the event overlaps with this working day
      AND e.start_timestamp < wd.day_date + TIME '18:00:00'
      AND e.end_timestamp > wd.day_date + TIME '09:00:00'
),
valid_segments AS (
    -- Calculate actual working time for each segment
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        resource_key,
        -- Only count if segment_start is before segment_end
        CASE 
            WHEN segment_start < segment_end 
            THEN EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (segment_end - segment_start))::bigint
            ELSE 0 
        END AS working_seconds
    FROM event_day_segments
)
SELECT 
    learner_code, 
    resource_key, 
    SUM(working_seconds) AS total_time_spent
FROM valid_segments
WHERE working_seconds > 0
GROUP BY learner_code, resource_key

WITH overall_avg AS (

JOIN latest_team_speeds lts ON m.home_team_api_id = lts.team_api_id AND lts.rn = 1

WITH match_dates AS (
    SELECT 
        "id",
        DATE(TO_TIMESTAMP("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')) AS match_date,
        TO_TIMESTAMP("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') AS match_start_time,
        TO_TIMESTAMP("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') + INTERVAL '2 hours' AS match_end_time,
        EXTRACT(DOW FROM TO_TIMESTAMP("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')) AS day_of_week
    FROM "match"
)
SELECT 
    m."id",
    m.match_start_time,
    m.match_end_time,
    ROUND(
        GREATEST(
            LEAST(m.match_end_time, m.match_date + TIME '17:00:00') - 
            GREATEST(m.match_start_time, m.match_date + TIME '07:30:00'),
            INTERVAL '0 minutes'
        ) / INTERVAL '1 minute'
    ) AS match_duration_minutes
FROM match_dates m
WHERE m.day_of_week BETWEEN 1 AND 5  -- Monday to Friday (1=Monday, 5=Friday)
    AND m.match_start_time < m.match_date + TIME '17:00:00'  -- Match starts before 5 PM
    AND m.match_end_time > m.match_date + TIME '07:30:00'    -- Match ends after 7:30 AM

WITH school_data AS (
  SELECT
    s.cdscode AS "pk",
    CASE
      WHEN s.charter = 1 THEN 'Charter'
      WHEN s.magnet = 1 THEN 'Magnet'
      ELSE 'Regular'
    END AS "col2",
    SUBSTRING(s.county FROM 1 FOR POSITION(' ' IN s.county) - 1) AS "col3",
    TO_TIMESTAMP(TO_CHAR(s.lastupdate, 'YYYY/MM/DD') || ' ' ||
           LPAD(CAST(MOD(ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY s.cdscode), 24) AS TEXT), 2, '0') || ':' ||
           LPAD(CAST(MOD(ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY s.cdscode) * 7, 60) AS TEXT), 2, '0'),
           'YYYY/MM/DD HH24:MI') AS "col4"
  FROM schools s
  WHERE s.statustype = 'Active'
    AND s.lastupdate IS NOT NULL
),
filtered_data AS (
  SELECT
    sd."pk",
    sd."col2",
    sd."col3",
    sd."col4"
  FROM school_data sd
  WHERE sd."col4" >= TO_TIMESTAMP('2024/09/04 00:00', 'yyyy/mm/dd hh24:mi')
    AND sd."col4" < TO_TIMESTAMP('2024/09/04 00:05', 'yyyy/mm/dd hh24:mi')
),
aggregated_data AS (
  SELECT
    fd."col2",
    STRING_AGG(DISTINCT fd."col3", ', ' ORDER BY fd."col3") AS "col3_list",
    COUNT(fd."pk") AS "record_count"
  FROM filtered_data fd
  GROUP BY fd."col2"
)

SELECT
  ad."col2",
  ad."col3_list" AS "col3",
  ad."record_count"
FROM aggregated_data ad
ORDER BY ad."record_count" DESC

WITH player_data AS (

        p.player_api_id AS player_id,
        'name' AS attribute_type,
        p.player_name AS attribute_value

    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        pa.player_api_id AS player_id,
        'rating' AS attribute_type,
        CAST(pa.overall_rating AS TEXT) AS attribute_value
    FROM player_attributes pa
    WHERE pa.date = (
        SELECT MAX(date)
        FROM player_attributes
        WHERE player_api_id = pa.player_api_id
    )
    AND pa.overall_rating IS NOT NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        pa.player_api_id AS player_id,
        'foot' AS attribute_type,
        pa.preferred_foot AS attribute_value
    FROM player_attributes pa
    WHERE pa.date = (
        SELECT MAX(date)
        FROM player_attributes
        WHERE player_api_id = pa.player_api_id
    )
    AND pa.preferred_foot IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
    player_id,
    MAX(attribute_value) FILTER (WHERE attribute_type = 'name') AS name,
    MAX(attribute_value) FILTER (WHERE attribute_type = 'rating') AS rating,
    MAX(attribute_value) FILTER (WHERE attribute_type = 'foot') AS preferred_foot
FROM player_data
GROUP BY player_id
ORDER BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(attribute_value) FILTER (WHERE attribute_type = 'rating') IS NULL 
        THEN 0 
        ELSE CAST(MAX(attribute_value) FILTER (WHERE attribute_type = 'rating') AS NUMERIC)

WITH categorized AS (
    SELECT 
        count,
        CASE 
            WHEN count >= 0 AND count < 21 THEN '0-21'
            WHEN count >= 21 AND count < 34 THEN '21-34'
            WHEN count >= 34 AND count < 64 THEN '34-64'
            WHEN count >= 64 THEN '64+'
        END AS count_range,
        CASE 
            WHEN count >= 0 AND count < 21 THEN 1
            WHEN count >= 21 AND count < 34 THEN 2
            WHEN count >= 34 AND count < 64 THEN 3
            WHEN count >= 64 THEN 4
        END AS range_order
    FROM tags
)
SELECT 
    count_range,
    AVG(count) AS avg_count
FROM categorized
GROUP BY count_range, range_order
ORDER BY range_order

WITH player_date_ranges AS (
    SELECT
        player_api_id,
        MIN(date::date) AS first_date,
        MAX(date::date) AS last_date
    FROM player_attributes
    GROUP BY player_api_id
),
gaps AS (
    -- Gap before first activity
    SELECT
        player_api_id,
        'Before First Date' AS gap_type,
        first_date AS relevant_date,
        (first_date - '2000-01-01'::date) AS days_gap
    FROM player_date_ranges
    WHERE first_date > '2000-01-01'::date
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Gap after last activity
    SELECT
        player_api_id,
        'After Last Date' AS gap_type,
        last_date AS relevant_date,
        ('2025-12-31'::date - last_date) AS days_gap
    FROM player_date_ranges
    WHERE last_date < '2025-12-31'::date
)
SELECT
    p.player_api_id,
    p.player_name,
    g.gap_type,
    g.relevant_date,
    g.days_gap
FROM gaps g
JOIN player p ON g.player_api_id = p.player_api_id
ORDER BY p.player_api_id, g.gap_type

UPDATE event
SET notes = (
    SELECT e2.notes 
    FROM event e2

WITH match_parsed AS (
    SELECT
        "date",
        "match_api_id",
        CAST("date" AS TIMESTAMP) AS match_timestamp,
        DATE(CAST("date" AS TIMESTAMP)) AS match_date,
        EXTRACT(HOUR FROM CAST("date" AS TIMESTAMP)) * 60 + 
        EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM CAST("date" AS TIMESTAMP)) AS minutes_from_midnight
    FROM "match"
),
with_target_distances AS (
    SELECT
        mp.*,
        8 * 60 AS morning_target_minutes,
        20 * 60 AS evening_target_minutes,
        ABS(mp.minutes_from_midnight - 8 * 60) AS morning_distance,
        ABS(mp.minutes_from_midnight - 20 * 60) AS evening_distance
    FROM match_parsed mp
),
morning_matches AS (
    SELECT
        match_date,
        "date" AS match_datetime,
        "match_api_id",
        morning_distance AS time_distance_minutes,
        'morning' AS time_period,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY match_date 
            ORDER BY morning_distance, "match_api_id"
        ) AS rn
    FROM with_target_distances
),
evening_matches AS (
    SELECT
        match_date,
        "date" AS match_datetime,
        "match_api_id",
        evening_distance AS time_distance_minutes,
        'evening' AS time_period,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY match_date 
            ORDER BY evening_distance, "match_api_id"
        ) AS rn
    FROM with_target_distances
)
SELECT
    match_date,
    time_period,
    match_datetime,
    "match_api_id",
    time_distance_minutes
FROM morning_matches
WHERE rn = 1
UNION ALL
SELECT
    match_date,
    time_period,
    match_datetime,
    "match_api_id",
    time_distance_minutes
FROM evening_matches
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY match_date, time_period

WITH RECURSIVE "atom_hierarchy" AS (
    -- Base case: start from 'TR007_8'
    SELECT 
        "atom_id", 
        "atom_id2", 
        "bond_id", 
        1 AS "level",
        ARRAY["atom_id"]::text[] AS "visited"  -- Track visited nodes
    FROM connected 
    WHERE "atom_id" = 'TR007_8'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: find next connected items, avoiding cycles
    SELECT 
        c."atom_id", 
        c."atom_id2", 
        c."bond_id", 
        ah."level" + 1,
        ah."visited" || c."atom_id"  -- Add current node to visited list
    FROM connected AS c 
    JOIN atom_hierarchy AS ah ON c."atom_id" = ah."atom_id2"
    WHERE 
        -- Prevent cycles: only proceed if we haven't visited this node before
        NOT c."atom_id2" = ANY(ah."visited")
        AND ah."level" < 100  -- Safety limit to prevent runaway queries
)
SELECT 
    "atom_id", 
    "atom_id2", 
    "bond_id", 
    "level" 
FROM atom_hierarchy 
ORDER BY "level" NULLS FIRST, "atom_id" NULLS FIRST

-- Create unique index to enforce condition 1: same (match_id, team_id) must have same score
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_match_team_score ON match_results (match_id, team_id, score);

-- Trigger function for condition 2: unique (match_id, team_id) pairs must have globally unique scores
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION enforce_unique_score_for_singletons()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
DECLARE
    current_count INTEGER;
BEGIN
    -- Count existing rows with same match_id and team_id (excluding current row for updates)
    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO current_count
    FROM match_results
    WHERE match_id = NEW.match_id 
      AND team_id = NEW.team_id
      AND id IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.id;
    
    -- If this (match_id, team_id) will be unique (count = 0), enforce score uniqueness
    IF current_count = 0 THEN
        -- Check if this score is used by another unique (match_id, team_id) combination
        IF EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 FROM match_results mr
            WHERE mr.score = NEW.score
              AND (mr.match_id != NEW.match_id OR mr.team_id != NEW.team_id)
              -- Ensure the other (match_id, team_id) is unique (only one row)
              AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM match_results t 
                   WHERE t.match_id = mr.match_id AND t.team_id = mr.team_id) = 1
        ) THEN
            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Score % is already assigned to another unique (match_id, team_id) pair', NEW.score;
        END IF;
    END IF;
    
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Create the trigger
DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_unique_score_for_singletons ON match_results;
CREATE TRIGGER trg_unique_score_for_singletons
    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON match_results
    FOR EACH ROW
    EXECUTE FUNCTION enforce_unique_score_for_singletons()

create or replace function setup_vector_store(table_name text) returns void language plpgsql as $outer$
declare
    match_func_name text;
begin
    -- Create dynamic function name
    match_func_name := 'match_' || table_name;
    
    -- Drop existing function if exists
    execute format('drop function if exists %I(int, vector, jsonb)', match_func_name);
    
    -- Create table with embedding column for vector similarity
    execute format('create table if not exists %I ( 
        id bigint primary key generated always as identity, 
        description text, 
        metadata jsonb,
        embedding vector(1536)
    )', table_name);
    
    -- Create dynamic match function with proper parameters
    execute format($func$
        create function %I(match_count int default null, query_embedding vector default null, filter jsonb default '{}'::jsonb) 
        returns table (id bigint, description text, metadata jsonb, similarity float) 
        language plpgsql as $inner$
        begin
            return query 
            select 
                t.id, 
                t.description, 
                t.metadata, 
                1 - (t.embedding <=> query_embedding) as similarity 
            from %I t
            where t.metadata @> filter 
            order by t.embedding <=> query_embedding 
            limit match_count;
        end;
        $inner$;
    $func$, match_func_name, table_name);
end;
$outer$

  WHERE dt < '2014-12-31'
),

  -- Identify all non-working days in the date range
  SELECT dt
  FROM date_range
  WHERE 
    -- Weekends (Saturday = 6, Sunday = 0 in PostgreSQL EXTRACT(DOW FROM date))
    EXTRACT(DOW FROM dt) IN (0, 6)
    -- Specific holidays
    OR (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM dt) = 1 AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM dt) = 1)   -- New Year's Day
    OR (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM dt) = 7 AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM dt) = 4)   -- Independence Day
    OR (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM dt) = 11 AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM dt) = 11) -- Veterans Day

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Build adjacency list with child info
Tree AS (
    SELECT 
        t.entityid,
        t.parentid,
        t.emission,
        t.pctcontribution,
        ARRAY(SELECT entityid FROM tree_table c WHERE c.parentid = t.entityid) AS children
    FROM tree_table t
),
-- Iterative processing: at each step, process nodes whose children are all done
Process AS (
    -- Start with leaves (empty children array or null)
    SELECT 
        entityid,
        parentid,
        emission,
        pctcontribution,
        emission::numeric AS cumulative_emission,
        ARRAY[entityid] AS done,
        0 AS step
    FROM Tree
    WHERE children IS NULL OR array_length(children, 1) = 0
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Process parents when all children are in done list
    SELECT 
        t.entityid,
        t.parentid,
        t.emission,
        t.pctcontribution,
        (t.emission + COALESCE(SUM(
            CASE WHEN c.entityid = ANY(t.children) 
                 THEN p.cumulative_emission * c.pctcontribution / 100.0 
                 ELSE 0 END
        ), 0))::numeric,
        p.done || t.entityid,
        p.step + 1
    FROM Tree t
    JOIN Process p ON t.entityid = ANY(p.done) = false  -- t not yet processed
    JOIN LATERAL (
        SELECT * FROM tree_table WHERE entityid = ANY(t.children)
    ) c ON true
    WHERE t.children <@ p.done  -- all children are done
    GROUP BY t.entityid, t.parentid, t.emission, t.pctcontribution, p.done, p.step
)
SELECT entityid, parentid, cumulative_emission AS total_emission
FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (entityid) entityid, parentid, cumulative_emission
    FROM Process
    ORDER BY entityid, step DESC
) sub
ORDER BY entityid

        got,
        COUNT(got) OVER (
            PARTITION BY id 
            ORDER BY date
        ) AS grp
    FROM laboratory
) sub
ORDER BY id, date

WITH "credits" AS (
    SELECT
        5001 AS "ID",
        12 AS "ARTCRED",
        7.5 AS "BIZCRED",
        12 AS "HUMCRED",
        14 AS "NATCRED",
        11 AS "TEKCRED",

    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        5001 AS "ID",
        15 AS "ARTCRED",
        9 AS "BIZCRED",
        14 AS "HUMCRED",
        16 AS "NATCRED",
        13 AS "TEKCRED",
        11 AS "GENCRED",
        TO_DATE('01.01.2020', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS "EFFECTIVE_DATE"
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        5002 AS "ID",

        4 AS "NATCRED",

),

"grp_choices" AS (
    SELECT
        5001 AS "ID",
        'ART' AS "GROUPCHOICE",
        TO_DATE('03.03.2010', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS "DATECHOICE"
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        5001 AS "ID",
        'BIZ' AS "GROUPCHOICE",
        TO_DATE('05.05.2015', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS "DATECHOICE"
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        5001 AS "ID",
        'NAT' AS "GROUPCHOICE",
        TO_DATE('23.06.2022', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS "DATECHOICE"
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        5002 AS "ID",
        'ART' AS "GROUPCHOICE",
        TO_DATE('23.06.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS "DATECHOICE"
),

-- Find the most recent credit record effective at the time of each choice
"effective_credits" AS (
    SELECT
        G."ID",
        G."GROUPCHOICE",
        G."DATECHOICE",
        C."ARTCRED",
        C."BIZCRED",
        C."HUMCRED",
        C."NATCRED",
        C."TEKCRED",
        C."GENCRED",
        C."EFFECTIVE_DATE",
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY G."ID", G."GROUPCHOICE", G."DATECHOICE" 
            ORDER BY C."EFFECTIVE_DATE" DESC
        ) AS RN
    FROM "grp_choices" G
    JOIN "credits" C 
        ON G."ID" = C."ID" 
        AND C."EFFECTIVE_DATE" <= G."DATECHOICE"
)

SELECT
    "ID",
    "GROUPCHOICE",
    "DATECHOICE",
    CASE "GROUPCHOICE"
        WHEN 'ART' THEN "ARTCRED"
        WHEN 'BIZ' THEN "BIZCRED"
        WHEN 'HUM' THEN "HUMCRED"
        WHEN 'NAT' THEN "NATCRED"
        WHEN 'TEK' THEN "TEKCRED"
        WHEN 'GEN' THEN "GENCRED"
    END AS "CREDIT_VALUE",
    "EFFECTIVE_DATE" AS "CREDIT_EFFECTIVE_DATE"
FROM "effective_credits"
WHERE RN = 1
ORDER BY "ID", "DATECHOICE"

WITH tbl AS
  (SELECT TO_DATE('12.01.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS act_date,
          1 AS rn
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('19.01.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 2
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('19.01.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 3
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('19.01.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 4
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('19.01.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 5
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('12.02.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 6
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('31.01.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 7
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('13.02.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 8
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('17.02.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 9
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('17.02.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 10
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('19.02.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 11
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('10.03.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 12
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('12.03.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 13
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('27.03.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 14
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
   UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('10.04.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy'), 15
   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy),
     t2 AS
  (SELECT TO_CHAR(t.act_date, 'MON-yy') AS act_month,
          TO_CHAR(t.act_date, 'yyyymm') AS rn_2
   FROM tbl t
   GROUP BY TO_CHAR(t.act_date, 'MON-yy'),
            TO_CHAR(t.act_date, 'yyyymm')),
     grid AS
  (SELECT t1.activation_month,
          t1.rn_1,
          t2.act_month,
          t2.rn_2
   FROM
     (SELECT TO_CHAR(t.act_date, 'MON-yy') AS activation_month,
             TO_CHAR(t.act_date, 'yyyymm') AS rn_1
      FROM tbl t
      GROUP BY TO_CHAR(t.act_date, 'MON-yy'),
               TO_CHAR(t.act_date, 'yyyymm')) t1
   LEFT JOIN t2 ON (t1.rn_1 <= t2.rn_2)),
     molecule_activity AS
  (SELECT m.molecule_id,
          TO_CHAR(t.act_date, 'yyyymm') AS act_date
   FROM (SELECT m.molecule_id, 
                ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY m.molecule_id) as rn
         FROM molecule m) m
   JOIN tbl t ON m.rn = t.rn)
SELECT g.activation_month,
       g.act_month,
       CASE
           WHEN g.rn_1 = g.rn_2 THEN
                  (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT ma.molecule_id)
                   FROM molecule_activity ma
                   WHERE ma.act_date = g.rn_1)
           ELSE
                  (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT ma.molecule_id)
                   FROM molecule_activity ma
                   WHERE ma.act_date BETWEEN g.rn_1 AND g.rn_2)
       END AS active_molecules
FROM grid g
ORDER BY g.rn_1,
         g.rn_2
